The Amalfi Coast is a World Heritage Site, and was rated by the American magazine "National Geographic" as one of the 51 beautiful places you must visit in your lifetime. Here there are winding coastlines and spectacular cliffs, as well as hidden medieval fishing towns, described as "highly dramatic." There are ancient medieval towns here, famous for their unique culture and architecture. Popular towns include Amalfi, Positano, Ravello and Maiori. In these towns you can see many ancient churches, castles, bell towers and temples, buildings whose history can be traced back centuries, each with its own story.
highlights.text_zh:
【人间天堂】：阿马尔菲海岸
阿马尔菲海岸是一处世界文化遗产，更被美国《国家地理》杂志评为一生中必须去的51个美丽的地方之一。这里有蜿蜒的海岸线和壮观的悬崖峭壁，还隐藏着中世纪渔业小镇，被形容为“高度戏剧性”。这里有着古老的中世纪小镇，这些小镇以其独特的文化和建筑而著名。受欢迎的小镇包括阿马尔菲、波西塔诺、拉韦洛和莱马尼。在这些小镇中，你可以看到许多古老的教堂、城堡、钟楼和神殿，这些建筑物的历史可以追溯到几个世纪以前，每个建筑都有着自己的故事。

attractions.text_en: Positano is a small town located along the Amalfi Coast in the Campania region of Italy. The main part of the town backs onto the mountains and faces the sea. One writer described it thus: Positano is a dreamland; while you are there, she is not very real, but after you leave, she becomes vivid and lifelike. The Amalfi Coast is hailed as the beautiful seashore of Europe; here the coastline winds and twists, towering mountains and islands stand majestically on the shore, valleys and headlands interlace, orchards and wild plants coexist, and within the pristine natural environment there is rich human history. Scattered among the dense mountain forests are many picturesque towns, and the more beautiful one among them is called Positano.
attractions.text_zh: 波西塔诺是位于意大利坎帕尼亚大区阿马尔菲海岸‎沿岸的一个小镇。城镇主要部份背山面海，一位作家这样形容：波西塔诺是一个梦乡，你在时，她不是很真切，你离开后，她变的栩栩如生。阿马尔菲海岸被誉为欧洲美丽的海滩，这里的海岸线蜿蜒曲折，岸上山岛耸峙巍峨，山谷与海岬交错，果园与野生植物并存，原生态的自然环境中有丰富的人文历史，浓密山林中散落着许多如诗如画的小镇，而其中较漂亮的那一个叫做波西塔诺。

attractions.text_en: It is located on the coast of the Province of Salerno in the Campania region of southern Italy; the small town close to the coast is called Amalfi, hence the name. Looking at it as a whole, here one half is the sea and the other half is sheer cliff, and on the cliffs are winding mountain roads and scattered, well-arranged villas.
attractions.text_zh: 它位于意大利南部坎帕尼亚区萨莱诺省沿海，接近海岸的小镇叫阿马尔菲(Amalfi)，由此得名。整体看去，这里一半是大海，一半是绝壁，绝壁上是蜿蜒的盘山公路和错落有致的别墅。

overview.text_en: After breakfast, head to Pompeii to explore the ruins of the ancient city of Pompeii and learn about the once-flourishing Pompeian civilization. Afterward, go to Naples to tour the old town of Naples, the Royal Palace of Naples (exterior) and Castel Nuovo (exterior). In the afternoon, head to Rome, and then conclude this wonderful journey in Rome.
overview.text_zh: 早餐后，前往庞贝，探索庞贝古城遗址，了解曾经的庞贝文明。之后前往那不勒斯游览那不勒斯老城，那不勒斯皇宫（外观）、新堡（外观）。下午前往罗马，之后在罗马结束美好行程。

accommodation.text_en: No accommodation
accommodation.text_zh: 无住宿

highlights.text_en:
The ancient city of Pompeii | Asleep for a thousand years, still brilliant and glorious upon awakening
The ancient city of Pompeii is located in the Campania region near the city of Naples in southern Italy. It was once a small fishing village, but because it was close to an important Mediterranean port, with convenient transportation and a gathering of merchants, by the 8th century BC it had already developed into the second-largest city after the ancient city of Rome. However, in 79 BC, with the eruption of Mount Vesuvius, volcanic ash and scorching lava instantly buried the ancient city of Pompeii, and the disaster claimed the lives of countless living beings overnight. Only after more than a thousand years did the ancient city of Pompeii see the light of day again.
highlights.text_zh:
庞贝古城丨沉睡千年，醒时依旧灿烂辉煌
庞贝古城位于意大利南部那不勒斯市附近的坎帕尼亚大区，曾经是一个小渔村，由于靠近地中海的重要港口，交通便利，商贾云集，在公元前8世纪，就已经发展成了古罗马城的第二大城市。然而在公元前79年，随着维苏威火山的爆发，火山灰与炽热的岩浆瞬间掩埋了庞贝古城，灾难让无数生灵一夜之间丧生。历经一千多年之后，庞贝古城方得重见天日。

attractions.text_en: Located at the eastern end of the ancient city, the open-air theater was built around the 1st century BC and could hold nearly 20,000 people. It is one of the older amphitheaters of ancient Rome, more than 40 years earlier than the Colosseum of Rome. It has three tiers of spectator seating, and the upper tier still preserves the pillars that supported the canopy in those days.
attractions.text_zh: 位于古城东端的露天剧场建立于约公元前1世纪，可容纳近2万人，是古罗马圆形剧场中较古老的一个，比罗马的圆形竞技场还要早40余年，这里有三层观众席，上层仍保留着当年支撑顶棚的支柱。

attractions.text_en: The ancient city of Pompeii was once a small-to-medium town backing onto the mountains and facing the sea, destroyed in a single day by the great eruption of Mount Vesuvius to its northwest. The town's streets and houses were buried by volcanic ash, yet it completely preserved the living scenes of the Pompeians of more than two thousand years ago, and later this vast site was gradually excavated. In addition to various architectural ruins, there are also some shocking casts. The people who perished when the volcano erupted, their bodies wrapped in volcanic ash, decayed leaving only empty shells; archaeology injected plaster into these empty shells to make casts, displaying to the world the tragic state of Pompeii at that time.
attractions.text_zh: 庞贝古城曾是一座背山面海的中小城镇，因西北边的维苏威火山大爆发而毁于一旦。城镇街道、房屋被火山灰掩埋，却完整保留了两千年多前庞贝人的生活场景，后来这座庞大的遗址才被渐渐发掘出来。除各种建筑遗迹外，还有一些触目惊心的铸像。在火山爆发时遇难的人，因被火山灰包裹而身体腐朽得只剩空壳，考古学把这些空壳注入石膏、做成铸像，向世人展示庞贝当时的惨状。

attractions.text_en: This is a historical site within the ancient city of Pompeii. It was once a temple, but because of its great age only ruined walls remain; still, the overall outline of the temple can still be made out.
attractions.text_zh: 这是庞贝古城内的一处历史遗址，这里曾经是一座寺庙，但因为年代久远只剩下残垣断壁了，但寺庙的整体轮廓还是看得出的。

attractions.name_en: The Forum at the Temple of Apollo
attractions.text_en: Also called the public square (Forum), this was once the political, economic and religious center of old Pompeii. The square's rectangular basilica still retains some brick column bases; this was formerly the law court and commercial trading venue. Opposite the basilica stands a Temple of Apollo, whose 48 Corinthian columns are well preserved, along with many exquisite frescoes. On the north side of the square is a Temple of Jupiter with its back to Mount Vesuvius, from which one can clearly gaze at Mount Vesuvius.
attractions.text_zh: 也称公共广场，这里曾经是昔日庞贝的政治、经济、宗教中心，广场上的方形会堂还残留着一些砖砌的圆柱底座，此处是以前的法院与商业交易场所。会堂的对面是一座阿波罗神殿，里面的48根科林斯式圆柱保存完好，还有许多精美的壁画。广场北侧有一座背对着维苏威火山的丘比特神殿，可以清楚的眺望维苏威火山。

attractions.text_en: For most travelers, the sea, the food and the history of Naples are all important reasons that make people reluctant to leave. Naples was the most beloved city of the Italian writer Boccaccio; it is the birthplace of the songs "'O Sole Mio" and "Come Back to Sorrento," and also the origin of pizza. Naples presents a different face throughout the four seasons of the year to travelers from all over the world. Naples is also called "Napoli," which is "Napoli" in Italian. Naples is the capital of the Campania region and the Province of Naples, and is also one of the most populous cities in Italy, abounding in lemons and grapes. At the same time, she is an important Mediterranean tourist city, known as the "City of Sunshine and Joy." This is a source of inspiration for many artists and musicians, perhaps because of Mount Vesuvius, or perhaps because of the simplicity of Naples.
attractions.text_zh: 对于大多数旅行者而言，那不勒斯的大海、美食及历史都是让人流连忘返的重要原因。那不勒斯是意大利作家薄伽丘最热爱的城市，这里是歌曲《我的太阳》、《重归苏莲托》的诞生地，也是披萨的起源地。那不勒斯一年四季都呈现出不同的面孔展现给来自世界各地的旅行者。那不勒斯也叫“拿波里”，意大利文是“Napoli”。那不勒斯是坎帕尼亚大区以及那不勒斯省的首府，也是意大利人口最多的城市之一，盛产柠檬和葡萄。同时，她也是地中海重要的旅游城市，被称为“阳光和欢乐之城”。这里是许多艺术家、音乐家的灵感之源，或许是因为维苏威火山(MountVesuvius)，又或许是因为那不勒斯的古朴。

attractions.text_en: Castel Nuovo, also known as Castello Angioino (Angevin Castle), was ordered built by the first king of the Kingdom of Naples. Castel Nuovo includes a civic museum (Museo Civico) as well as the castle ruins. The museum (mainly displaying paintings) is on the first floor, from the second floor one can see sea views, and the ground floor is the ruins of Castel Nuovo, with the surface built of transparent glass; travelers with a fear of heights should pay special attention, as beneath the glass are the ruins of ancient buildings and human skeletons. In addition, inside Castel Nuovo there is also a Sala Dei Baroni (literally "Hall of the Barons"), the architecture at the top of the room being very distinctive.
attractions.text_zh: 新堡，又名安茹城堡，是那不勒斯王国第一位国王下令建立。新堡包括公民博物馆(MuseoCivico)以及城堡遗迹。博物馆(主要展示绘画)位于第一层，第二层可以看到海景，零层则为新堡遗迹，地表为透明玻璃建造，有恐高症的旅行者需要特别注意，玻璃底下则为古建筑遗迹以及人的骸骨。另外，新堡内部还有一个SalaDeiBaroni(直译为男爵的房间)，房间顶端的建筑非常有特色。

attractions.text_en: The Royal Palace of Naples sits in the bustling downtown, close to famous attractions such as Castel Nuovo and Castel dell'Ovo. Having gone through four dynasties, its exterior looks very ordinary, but if you choose to go inside to visit, it will surely amaze you. This palace is extremely luxurious; the overall color scheme is basically gold, yellow and purple, representing the nobility of royalty. The palace has many, many rooms—the queen's bedchamber, the council chamber, the study, and so on—and inside there are also many displays of royal items and paintings of the royal nobility. The most noteworthy is the throne. The most worth-seeing place inside this palace, apart from the various paintings, is it; words such as noble and elegant cannot simply describe it.
attractions.text_zh: 那不勒斯皇宫身处闹市，临近新堡、蛋堡等知名景点，历经四个朝代，外表看上去十分普通，但是，如果选择入内参观，那么一定会让你大吃一惊，这个皇宫非常奢华，整个色调基本就是金色、黄色、紫色，代表皇室的高贵。皇宫有许许多多的房间，王后的寝宫、议政厅、书房等等，里面还有很多皇室用品的展示和皇室贵族的绘画，最值得一提的就是王座。这个皇宫里面最值得一看的地方，除了各类绘画就是它了，用尊贵典雅这一类词是无法简单形容的。

attractions.text_en: Located in the heart of Rome, the spectacular Colosseum was built by ancient Rome to please the generals and soldiers returning in triumph and to glorify the great Roman Empire. It was a place reserved for slave owners, nobles and free citizens of ancient Rome to watch beast fights or gladiatorial combat between slaves, and it is a famous historical relic of Rome. The Colosseum can be called a masterpiece and a marvel in the history of architecture, renowned throughout the world for being huge, majestic and spectacular. Although only most of its skeleton remains, its grand spirit and magnificent momentum still endure.
attractions.text_zh: 位于罗马中心的壮观的斗兽场是古罗马当时为取悦凯旋而归的将领士兵和赞美伟大的古罗马帝国而建造的，是古罗马帝国专供奴隶主、贵族和自由民观看斗兽或奴隶角斗的地方，是罗马著名的历史遗迹。斗兽场在建筑史上堪称典范的杰作和奇迹，以庞大、雄伟、壮观著称于世。虽只剩下大半个骨架，但其雄伟之气魄、磅礴之气势犹存。

attractions.text_en: The Pantheon is a well-preserved building of ancient Rome, with a history of more than 2,000 years. The artist Raphael, the Roman kings Vittorio Emanuele II and Umberto I, and others rest here in eternal sleep. The Pantheon was built by Agrippa, son-in-law of Octavian, the first emperor of the Roman Empire, to honor the gods of Mount Olympus. It can be called a classic building of the Augustan period and a model of the exquisite architectural techniques of ancient Rome, praised by Michelangelo as "the design of angels."
attractions.text_zh: 万神殿是古罗马保存完好的建筑，距今已有2000多年的历史，艺术家拉斐尔、罗马皇帝维托里奥·埃曼努埃尔二世和温布尔一世等长眠于此。万神殿由罗马帝国首任皇帝屋大维的女婿阿戈利巴建造，用以供奉奥林匹亚山上诸神，可谓奥古斯都时期的经典建筑，是古罗马精湛建筑技术的典范，被米开朗基罗赞叹为“天使的设计”。

attractions.text_en: Piazza di Spagna once appeared in scenes of the film "Roman Holiday," becoming a calling card of Rome known to everyone. Hepburn's fresh and bright figure seems to have merged with the Spanish Square and beautiful Rome, becoming a memory that people can never dispel. The Spanish Steps: the whole Spanish Square is not large in area, and the crowds are mainly concentrated near the Spanish Steps. The steps have a total of 138 levels and are among the widest steps in Europe.
attractions.text_zh: 西班牙广场曾经出现在电影《罗马假日》的场景中，成为了所有人都知道的罗马名片。赫本那清新亮丽的倩影似乎已经与西班牙广场以及美丽的罗马合为一体，成为了人们永远挥之不去的记忆。西班牙台阶整个西班牙广场面积不大，人群主要集中在西班牙台阶附近。整个台阶共138级，是欧洲较宽的台阶。

attractions.name_zh: 特莱维喷泉(许愿池)（外观）
attractions.text_en: Also known as the "Maiden Fountain" or the "Wishing Fountain," it takes as its theme the triumphant return of Neptune, the sea god in Roman mythology, full of grandeur and majesty. It is said that if you turn your back to the fountain and toss a coin over your shoulder, and it lands in the water, your dream will come true.
attractions.text_zh: 又称“少女喷泉”，或者“许愿池”，以罗马神话中海神尼普勒战胜归来为题材，整气势磅礴、大气恢宏。据说背对着喷泉、从肩上投出一枚硬币，如果能投进水中，就能梦想成真。

attractions.text_en: The winding cypress trail, a country road lined on both sides with Italian cypresses, with its uniquely shaped curves and neatly arranged cypress trees, has become one of the symbols of the pastoral scenery of Tuscany. The cypresses on both sides of the road cast dappled shadows in the sunshine, creating a quiet and poetic atmosphere that attracts countless photography enthusiasts and tourists to come and take photos.
attractions.text_zh: 蜿蜒曲折丝柏树小径、两旁种满意大利柏树的乡村道路，独特造型的弯道和整齐排列的丝柏树，成为托斯卡纳田园风光的象征之一。道路两侧的柏树在阳光的照射下投下斑驳的影子，营造出一种宁静而诗意的氛围，吸引了无数摄影爱好者和游客前来打卡。

attractions.text_en: Pienza, a small town on a hill in the Val d'Orcia in southern Tuscany, is the birthplace of Pope Pius II. The Pienza that he oversaw rebuilding in 1459 is regarded as a model of the "ideal city," and is therefore hailed as a "touchstone of Renaissance urban life." Unlike other medieval Italian towns, Pienza's unique feature is that from its city walls one can overlook the scenery of the World Heritage site "Val d'Orcia."
attractions.text_zh: 皮恩扎，位于托斯卡尼南部奥尔恰山谷山丘上的小镇，这里是教皇皮乌斯二世的出生地，1459年他主持重建的皮恩扎被视为“理想城市”的典范，因此被誉为“文艺复兴都市生活的试金石”。与其他意大利中世纪小镇不同，皮恩扎独特之处在于可以从城墙上俯瞰世界遗产-「Val D'orcia奥尔恰山谷」的景致。

attractions.text_en: Siena, this medieval old city, is filled with masterpieces of art masters and medieval classical architecture. Wandering through Siena's ancient and crisscrossing stone-paved alleys and the deep courtyards hidden everywhere, you feel as though you are in the European Middle Ages a thousand years ago, and every detail will bring you a surprise. This city has maintained its complete classical appearance for several centuries, as if time has stood still here since the fourteenth century. In addition to its unique scenery and art collections, Siena also maintains a series of traditions, including the making of desserts. On your trip to Siena, don't forget to taste the local specialties - almond soft cakes ricciarelli, nut tart panforte, peppery spiced nut cake pan pepato, rice pudding budini di riso, walnut cookies cavallucci, or walnut wafers copate!
attractions.text_zh: 锡耶纳，这座中世纪古城布满了艺术大师的杰作和中世纪古典建筑，穿梭在锡耶纳古老而纵横交错的石板小巷和隐藏在各处的幽深庭院中，您仿佛置身于千年前的欧洲中世纪，其中的每一处细节都会给您带来惊喜。这座城市在几个世纪以来，一直保持着完整的古典外貌，仿佛时间自十四世纪起便在此停滞。除了它独特的风景和艺术典藏，锡耶纳还保持着一系列的传统，包括甜品的烹饪制作。在您的锡耶纳之行中，别忘了品尝当地的特色- 杏仁软饼ricciarelli、果仁馅饼panforte、胡椒香料果仁糕pan pepato、米饭布丁budini di riso、核桃饼cavallucci、或核桃华夫copate!

attractions.text_en: Piazza del Campo (also called the Shell Square because of its shell-like shape) is the center of the city of Siena. Directly opposite the square are the Siena Town Hall and the tower. Inside the Town Hall is the Civic Museum, which houses many paintings of the Sienese School, including Ambrogio Lorenzetti's "Allegory of Good Government" and "Allegory of Bad Government." The tower's full name is the Torre del Mangia; it is the landmark building of Siena and also the second tallest tower in Italy, with a vertical height of 102 meters. Visitors can reach the top via 55 flights of stairs to enjoy a panoramic view of the beautiful city of Siena.
attractions.text_zh: 田野广场(因形似贝壳也叫贝壳广场)是锡耶纳城市的中心，广场正对面是锡耶纳市政厅和塔楼，市政厅内有市立博物馆，里面藏有锡耶纳学派的众多绘画作品，包括AmbrogioLorenzetti的《好政府寓意》和《坏政府寓意》。塔楼全名为曼贾塔TorrediMangia，它是锡耶纳标志性建筑，也是意大利第二高的塔楼，垂直高度达到102米，游客可以通过55层的阶梯达到顶端，一览锡耶纳城的美景。

attractions.text_en: Torre Grossa is the tower in the small town of San Gimignano that is open to the public, with a total height of 54 meters; its construction began in the early 14th century. It was once part of the new Palazzo del Podestà, and from the tower one can enjoy a panoramic view of San Gimignano and the hilly mountain region of Tuscany.
attractions.text_zh: 格罗萨塔楼是圣吉米亚诺小城对公众开放的塔楼，总高为54米，始建于14世纪初。它曾是新的波德斯塔宫宫的一部分，塔楼可以一览圣吉米亚诺和托斯卡纳丘陵山区的美景。

attractions.text_en: Although this medieval tower town listed as a World Heritage site is small, it concentrates the finest scenery of Tuscany. Piazza della Cisterna is the absolute core; this triangular square is paved with cobblestones and surrounded by 13th-century stone towers and arcades, and the ancient well in the center was once the lifeline of the whole town. Standing here and looking up at the densely packed towers, you can intuitively feel where the title "Medieval Manhattan" comes from.
attractions.text_zh: 这座被列入世界遗产的中世纪塔城虽小，却浓缩了托斯卡纳最精华的景观。水井广场是绝对核心，这个三角形广场由鹅卵石铺就，四周环绕着13世纪的石塔与拱廊，中央的古井曾是全城命脉。站在这里仰视林立的塔楼，能直观感受“中世纪曼哈顿”的称号从何而来。

attractions.text_en: Cinque Terre is located in the coastal area of the Province of La Spezia in the Liguria region of Italy, built along the winding Mediterranean coastline. It is the collective name for these five cliffside villages and towns: Monterosso al Mare, Vernazza, Corniglia, Manarola and Riomaggiore. What is special is the hiking route that strings the five villages together; built along the coast, it is one of the most beautiful hiking routes in the world, about 15-20 kilometers and taking around 5 hours. On one side is the Mediterranean, on the other side are the mountains, and in front are the Italian fishing villages, making it a place that many hiking enthusiasts flock to. In 1997 it was inscribed on the World Cultural Heritage List by UNESCO.
attractions.text_zh: 五渔村位于意大利利古里亚大区(Liguria)拉斯佩齐亚省海沿岸地区，沿着蜿蜒的地中海海岸线而建，是蒙特罗索（Monterosso al Mare）、韦尔纳扎（Vernazza）、科尔尼利亚（Corniglia）、马纳罗拉（Manarola）及里奥马焦雷（Riomaggiore）这五个悬崖边上的村镇的统称。特别的是把五个村庄串起的徒步线路，沿着海岸而建，是世界上美丽的徒步路线，约15-20公里，需5小时左右，一边是地中海，另一边是山脉，而前面则是意大利渔庄，是诸多徒步爱好者趋之若鹜的胜地。1997年被联合国教科文组织列入世界文化遗产名录。

attractions.text_en: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is the freestanding bell tower of the cathedral of the city of Pisa in Italy, located on the Piazza dei Miracoli on the north side of the city of Pisa in the Tuscany region of Italy. It is a world-famous landmark and one of the symbols of Italy, and has been designated a World Heritage site by UNESCO. According to legend, in 1590 AD, Galileo conducted a free-fall experiment on the Leaning Tower of Pisa and overturned the views of Aristotle.
attractions.text_zh: 比萨斜塔是意大利比萨城大教堂的独立式钟楼，位于意大利托斯卡纳省比萨城北面的奇迹广场上，是世界著名的景观，也是意大利标志之一，被联合国教育科学文化组织评选为世界遗产。相传在公元1590年，伽利略曾经在比萨斜塔上做了一个自由落体实验，并推翻了亚里士多德的观点

attractions.text_en: Also known as the Cathedral Square, it is a walled area in the center of the city of Pisa in the Tuscany region of Italy, partly grassland. The Piazza dei Miracoli is the center of the city of Pisa, and on the square there are four major buildings: the Pisa Cathedral, the Leaning Tower of Pisa (bell tower), the Baptistery of St. John, and the Pisa Cemetery (Camposanto). The square is also surrounded by ancient city walls and gates that still remain. In April 1987, the International Council on Monuments and Sites nominated the Piazza dei Miracoli of Pisa, Italy (including the cathedral, baptistery, Leaning Tower of Pisa and cemetery) as a World Heritage site.
attractions.text_zh: 又名主教座堂广场，是意大利托斯卡纳大区比萨市中心一片围墙包围的区域，部分为草地。奇迹广场是比萨城的中心，广场上有四大建筑：比萨主教座堂、比萨斜塔（钟楼）、圣乔瓦尼洗礼堂、比萨墓园。广场周围还保留着古代的城墙和城门。1987年4月，国际古迹遗产理事会提名意大利比萨城的奇迹广场（包括大教堂、洗礼堂、比萨斜塔和墓园）为世界遗产。

attractions.name_en: Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (entry requires queuing)
attractions.name_zh: 圣母百花大教堂(需排队进入)
attractions.text_en: There are many solemn and majestic churches in the world, but very few churches can be so enchanting. This beautiful church, faced with white, red and green granite, interprets to the fullest the classicism, elegance and freedom revered in the Renaissance era; no wonder it was named the "St. Mary of the Flower." The original site of the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore was the Church of Santa Reparata built in the 4th century. In 1296, Giovanni de' Medici funded the construction of the new church, which took 175 years to finally complete.
attractions.text_zh: 世界上庄严雄伟的教堂很多，但很少有教堂能如此妩媚。这座使用白、红、绿三色花岗岩贴面的美丽教堂将文艺复兴时代所推崇的古典、优雅、自由诠释得淋漓尽致，难怪会被命名为“花之圣母”。圣母百花大教堂原址是建于4世纪的圣·雷帕拉塔教堂。1296年，乔凡尼·美第奇出资建造新的教堂，花了175年时间才最终建成。

attractions.text_en: Piazza della Signoria has the reputation of being an open-air sculpture museum, and has also been the center of the city's political life for several centuries. On the square stand the Palazzo Vecchio (Old Palace), a replica of Michelangelo's sculpture "David," and the Fountain of Neptune. The open Piazza della Signoria is surrounded by the Uffizi Gallery, the Loggia dei Lanzi and numerous cafes and bars. The famous Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore is located directly to its north.
attractions.text_zh: 市政广场有露天雕塑博物馆的美誉，也是该城数个世纪以来政治生活中心，广场上坐落着维琪奥宫（旧宫）、米开朗基罗雕塑作品《大卫》的复制品以及海神喷泉。开放式的市政广场，周围环绕着乌菲兹美术馆、佣兵凉亭和众多的咖啡厅、酒吧。著名的圣母百花大教堂就位于它的正北方。

attractions.text_en: The Ponte Vecchio over the Arno River is also known as the Old Bridge. Built in 1345, it is one of the earliest large-span circular-arch bridges in Europe. It is said that Dante encountered the maiden Beatrice here, and later poured his feelings of longing into the "Divine Comedy." Over the centuries, it has experienced floods, social unrest and wars, yet has stood firm. It is one of the famous landmark buildings of Florence.
attractions.text_zh: 位于阿诺河(Arno)上的维奇奥桥，又被称为老桥。建于1345年，是欧洲较早的大跨度圆弧拱桥，据说但丁在这里邂逅了少女比阿特丽丝，后来将思慕之情倾注到《神曲》之中。几百年间，它经历过的水灾、社会动乱、战争而屹立不倒。是佛罗伦萨的著名地标建筑之一。

attractions.text_en: Located in the southeast of Florence, Piazzale Michelangelo is a famous spot for overlooking the beautiful panorama of the city. In the center of the square stands a replica of Michelangelo's immortal work - "David." From the square you can see the whole appearance of the city: the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore, Giotto's Bell Tower, the Bargello Museum, the Old Bridge and more are all in view. This is also a good location for viewing the sunrise and sunset; as the light changes, watch Florence radiate different splendor.
attractions.text_zh: 位于佛罗伦萨东南的米开朗基罗广场是著名的俯瞰城市美丽全景的地点，广场中央安放着米开朗基罗的不朽之作——《大卫》的复制品，广场上可以看到全城的风貌：圣母百花大教堂、乔托钟楼、巴杰罗美术馆、老桥等尽收眼底。这里也是观赏日出和日落的好位置，随着光线的变幻，看佛罗伦萨焕发出不同的光彩。

attractions.name_zh: 威尼斯圣马可大教堂 (外观)
attractions.text_en: Abbreviated as St. Mark's Basilica, it stands on St. Mark's Square in the center of Venice. First built in 829 AD and rebuilt in 1043-1071, it was once the largest church in medieval Europe. It is a classic work of Venetian architectural art, and it is also a treasure house holding a rich collection of artworks.
attractions.text_zh: 简称圣马可大教堂，是矗立于威尼斯市中心的圣马可广场上。始建于公元829年，重建于1043~1071年，它曾是中世纪欧洲大的教堂，是威尼斯建筑艺术的经典之作，它同时也是一座收藏丰富艺术品的宝库。

attractions.text_en: It is the central square of Venice, once called by Napoleon "the most beautiful drawing room in Europe." The square is surrounded on three sides by grand and magnificent palaces, while to the east rise the tall bell tower and St. Mark's Basilica. Since ancient times, it has been the center of public activity for Venice's politics, religion and traditional festivals.
attractions.text_zh: 是威尼斯的中心广场，曾被拿破仑称作“欧洲美丽的客厅”。广场三面被宏伟壮丽的宫殿围绕，东面耸立着高大的钟楼和圣马可教堂。自古以来，就是威尼斯政治、宗教及传统节日的公共活动中心。

attractions.text_en: Adjacent to St. Mark's Basilica, it was once the residence of the Doge of the Republic of Venice and the center of government. This Gothic building made of pink marble has an exquisitely beautiful exterior, with the details of its arcades and sculptures full of power and elegance, making it a symbol of Venetian politics and art.
attractions.text_zh: 紧邻圣马可大教堂，是昔日威尼斯共和国总督的官邸与政府中心。这座粉色大理石打造的哥特式建筑外观精美绝伦，拱廊与雕塑细节充满力量与优雅，是威尼斯政治与艺术的象征。

attractions.text_en: The Bridge of Sighs was in ancient times the only route by which condemned prisoners were escorted from the court to the prison. The Bridge of Sighs is in the early Baroque style. The bridge is shaped like a house, with the upper part covered by a dome and very tightly enclosed; only on the side facing the canal are there two small windows. After a prisoner had been tried in the Doge's Palace, the felons were taken to the dungeon. When passing over this airtight bridge, they could only look at the blue sky through the small windows, and from then on they lost their freedom, involuntarily letting out sounds of sighing. It is said that if lovers kiss under the bridge they can be together forever, and the film "A Little Romance" was shot here.
attractions.text_zh: 叹息桥是古代由法院向监狱押送死囚的必经之路。叹息桥造型属早期巴洛克式风格，桥呈房屋状，上部穹隆覆盖，封闭得很严实，只有向运河一侧有两个小窗，当犯人在总督府接受审判之后，重罪犯被带到地牢中，在经过这座密不透气的的桥时，只能透过小窗看看蓝天，从此失去了自由，不自主的发出叹息之声。听说恋人们在桥下接吻就可以天长地久，而电影「情定日落桥」就在这取景的。

attractions.text_en: Although its name Duomo means "dome," Milan Cathedral is actually a Gothic building. Dominating the city center, Milan Cathedral was first built in 1386 and took 500 years to complete. Praised as a magnificent Gothic building of Italy, its hundreds of spires soar into the clouds, and its white marble exterior walls are carved with thousands of statues. The interior space is vast, and sunlight pours in through the beautiful stained-glass mosaics on the windows, the light dim and hazy, full of a solemn religious atmosphere. In the crypt of the church there are also preserved the relics of the Church of St. Tecla that stood on the original site.
attractions.text_zh: 虽然它的名字Duomo是“圆顶”的意思，米兰大教堂实际上却是一座哥特式建筑。雄踞在市中心的米兰大教堂始建于1386年，历经500年才完工，被誉为意大利壮丽的哥特式建筑数以百计的尖塔耸入云霄，白色大理石外墙上雕刻着数千座雕像。内部空间宽广，阳光透过窗上美丽的彩色玻璃镶嵌画射入，光线朦胧，充满庄严的宗教气氛。教堂地下室中还保留着原址上的圣·特库拉教堂的遗迹。

attractions.text_en: This is a gorgeous shopping mall, although most tourists who come here come for sightseeing. Under the central arcade there is a Prada boutique, whose scale and window displays are among the best in Milan, especially the window displays which are updated daily; just standing outside the windows admiring them is a great pleasure. The Galleria Vittorio Emanuele is full of many high-end restaurants, cafes and shops.
attractions.text_zh: 这是一个华丽的购物商场，不过多数来这里的游客都是来观光的。中央拱廊下有着Prada精品店，规模和橱窗布置都是米兰数一数二的，尤其橱窗布置天天更新，光是站在橱窗外欣赏也是一大乐事。埃马努埃莱长廊遍布了不少高级的餐馆、咖啡厅以及商店。

attractions.text_en: Lake Zurich is 4 kilometers wide and about 39 kilometers long. The docks on the lake are full of yachts of all kinds, like a rich people's club. One end of Lake Zurich reaches deep into the city center; on the lake, white swans and wild ducks can be seen everywhere, leisurely and carefree, a harmonious scene. The north shore of Lake Zurich is the residence of the rich, known as the "Gold Coast," while the south shore is called the "Silver Coast." At the end of Bahnhofstrasse is Lake Zurich. As one of the "most livable cities in the world," the life of the true people of Zurich is permeated with tranquility and leisure everywhere.
attractions.text_zh: 苏黎世湖宽4公里，长约39公里，湖面码头停满了各色游艇，俨然一个富人俱乐部。苏黎世湖一段的尽头深入市中心，湖面上白天鹅、野鸭随处可见，悠闲自在，一派和谐景象。苏黎世湖的北岸为富人居住地，号称“金岸”，而南岸则称之为“银岸”。班霍夫大街的尽头便是苏黎世湖，作为“世界宜居的城市”，真正的苏黎世人的生活处处渗透着宁静和悠闲。

attractions.text_en: The twin towers of the Grossmünster are a landmark building of Zurich. According to legend, Charlemagne (Charlemagne the Great, king of the Carolingian dynasty of the Frankish Kingdom and founder of the Holy Roman Empire) built the twin towers on the graves of Zurich's patron saints Felix and Regula. Other sights worth seeing also include: St. Peter's Church, which has the largest clock face in Europe, and the Fraumünster of Zurich, which is famous for the stained-glass windows painted by Giacometti (the Swiss surrealist-existentialist master sculptor and painter) and Chagall (the surrealist painter).
attractions.text_zh: 苏黎世大教堂的双塔是苏黎世的地标性建筑。根据传说，查理曼（查理曼大帝，法兰克王国卡洛林王朝国王，神圣罗马帝国的奠基人）将双塔建在了苏黎世主保圣人Felix和Regula的墓地之上。其他值得一看的景点还包括：拥有欧洲较大钟面的圣彼得教堂和苏黎世圣母大教堂，该教堂因贾科梅蒂（瑞士超存在主义雕塑大师，画家）和夏卡尔（超现实主义画家）绘制的彩色玻璃窗而闻名。

attractions.text_en: The Old Town of Zurich is a not-to-be-missed place for an experience. Here are gathered traditional craftsmen and antique dealers, bookstores and flower shops, as well as jewelry, fashion boutiques, and of course no shortage of Zurich's local brands. Famous streets and lanes such as Bahnhofstrasse, Rennweg, Niederdorf and Augustine Lane are all located in the Old Town. Strolling through the Old Town at night, you will find that it has turned into an entertainment venue for the public.
attractions.text_zh: 苏黎世的老城区是个不可错过的体验之地，这里汇聚了传统工匠与古董商、书店和花铺，也有珠宝首饰，时尚精品，当然也少不了苏黎世本土品牌。班霍夫大街(Bahnhofstrasse)、伦韦格街(Rennweg)、尼德道尔夫(Niederdorf)与圣奥古斯丁巷(Augustine Lane)等著名街巷都位于老城区。晚上漫步于老城区，会发现这里又变成了民众的娱乐场所。

attractions.text_en: Lucerne is located in central Switzerland and belongs to the German-speaking region of Switzerland. Lucerne, nestled against mountains and water, possesses the beauty unique to the Middle Ages: medieval churches and towers, Renaissance-era palace halls and mansions, as well as century-old shops, long streets and ancient lanes, are everywhere. The lake and mountain scenery of Lake Lucerne reflects the beautiful scenery of the city in an almost dreamlike, almost real way, making it that extraordinary pearl among Switzerland's many beautiful small cities. Since the 19th century, it has attracted many princes, nobles and men of letters to come for sightseeing and to live, and artists have found endless artistic inspiration in Lucerne.
attractions.text_zh: 卢塞恩位于瑞士中部，属瑞士德语区。依山傍水的卢塞恩具有中世纪所特有的美：中世纪的教堂、塔楼，文艺复兴时期的宫厅、邸宅以及百年老店、长街古巷，比比皆是。卢塞恩湖的湖光山色更是将城中的美景倒影的亦幻亦真，是瑞士众多美丽小城中不凡的那颗明珠。从19世纪起，就吸引了众多的王公贵族和文人墨客前来游览和居住，艺术家们在卢塞恩得到了不尽的艺术灵感。

attractions.text_en: Switzerland's GoldenPass train shuttles through the beautiful regions of the Alps, taking in charming lake and mountain scenery all the way. One of the most beautiful sections is the GoldenPass Line train (Luzren-Lungern Express) from Lucerne (Luzern) to Lungern. Along the way are pretty villages, azure lake water, lush vegetation, vast pastures, leisurely cattle and sheep, as well as the snow-capped Alps. The scenery changing from the foot of the mountains to the summit makes one marvel that this is a fairyland on earth.
attractions.text_zh: 瑞士的黄金列车穿梭于阿尔卑斯美丽的区域，一路总览迷人的湖光山色。其中美丽的一段就是卢塞恩（Luzern）到龙疆（Lungern）这一段的金色山口列车（Luzren-Lungern Express），沿途秀美的村庄，碧蓝的湖水，茂密的植被，广袤的牧场，悠闲的牛羊，还有白雪皑皑的阿尔卑斯山脉，从山脚到山顶变幻的风景，让人感叹这就是人间仙境。

attractions.text_en: Lake Lucerne is winding in shape, and together with the surrounding mountains it forms typical "lake and mountain" scenery, attracting large numbers of tourists to come and sightsee. The music critic Ludwig Rellstab once described the hazy atmosphere of the first movement of Beethoven's "Piano Sonata No. 14" as the moonlight of Lake Lucerne, and this piece thus acquired the title "Moonlight" - although this was not Beethoven's original intention.
attractions.text_zh: 卢塞恩湖这座湖泊形状曲折，和周边的群山构成了典型的“湖光山色”景致，吸引了大量游客前来观光。音乐评论家雷尔斯塔布(Ludwig Rellstab)曾将贝多芬《第14钢琴奏鸣曲》的第1乐章的朦胧气氛形容为卢塞恩湖的月色，这首曲子因此得到了《月光》这个标题——虽然这并非贝多芬的本意。

attractions.text_en: Located between Lucerne and Interlaken, the famous GoldenPass Line train passes through here. This place has the purest alpine lake water in Switzerland, surrounded by mountains, with lush green grass, and the quaint villages scattered along the shores of Lake Lungern, just like a paradise far away from the hustle and bustle.
attractions.text_zh: 地处卢塞恩与因特拉肯之间，著名的金色山口列车就会从这里经过。这里拥有瑞士最为纯净的高山湖水，群山环抱，绿草茵茵，古朴的村庄散落于龙疆湖畔，俨然一座远离尘嚣的世外桃源。

attractions.text_en: This statue of a dying lion was carved on natural rock in 1821 by a Danish sculptor. This lion, 10 meters long and more than 3 meters high, lies in agony on the ground, a broken spear stuck in its shoulder, with a shield bearing the Swiss coat of arms beside it. This statue was made to commemorate the 786 Swiss mercenaries who all died on August 10, 1792, protecting the safety of the family of Louis XVI in the Tuileries Palace in Paris. Beneath the statue there is text describing the course of this event. In those days, Switzerland was a poor and backward country, and men, forced by livelihood, went to various European countries to serve as mercenaries one after another. Swiss mercenaries were loyal to their employers and brave in battle, but honor and money could not cover up the cruelty of the mercenary system. After this event, Switzerland stopped exporting mercenaries, retaining only the guards serving the Catholic Holy See, which is the famous Swiss Guard. Because of their famous loyalty and bravery, this Swiss Guard of the Holy See has served right up to the present. Later, the American writer Mark Twain came to Lucerne and praised the "dying lion of Lucerne" as "the most mournful and moving statue in the world."
attractions.text_zh: 这只濒死的狮子雕像是1821年由丹麦雕塑家雕刻在天然岩石上的。这头长10米、高3米多的雄狮，痛苦地倒在地上，折断的长矛插在肩头，旁边有一个带有瑞士国徽的盾牌。这座雕像是为了纪念1792年8月10日，为保护巴黎杜乐丽宫(Tuileries)中的路易十六家族的安全，全部战死的786名瑞士雇佣兵，雕像下方有文字描述了此事件的经过。当年，瑞士是一个贫穷落后的国家，男子迫于生计，纷纷到欧洲各国当雇佣兵。瑞士雇佣兵忠于雇主，英勇善战，但荣誉和金钱掩盖不了雇佣兵制度的残酷，这次事件之后，瑞士停止出口雇佣兵，仅留下为天主教廷服务的近卫军，就是那支著名的瑞士卫队。由于著名的忠勇，教廷的这支瑞士近卫军一直服务到现在。后来，美国作家马克·吐温来到卢塞恩，将“濒死的琉森狮子”誉为“世界上最悲壮和最感人的雕像”。

attractions.text_en: The Jungfrau is located twenty or thirty kilometers south of Interlaken, at an altitude of 4,158 meters, and is one of the higher peaks of the Alps. The summit is covered with ice and snow all year round, while at the foot of the mountain the grass is lush green and the scenery is beautiful, making it a famous sightseeing and outdoor sports destination in Switzerland. The Jungfrau region was inscribed as a World Natural Heritage site in 2001.
attractions.text_zh: 少女峰位于因特拉肯以南二三十公里处，海拔 4158 米，是阿尔卑斯山脉的较高的山峰之一。山顶常年被冰雪覆盖，而山下却绿草茵茵、风景秀丽，是瑞士有名的观光及户外运动胜地。少女峰地区已于 2001 年被列为世界自然遗产。

attractions.text_en: Located at the northern foot of the Eiger, Grindelwald is the place where photographers most love the famous postcard photo spot of Switzerland's "dreamy slope." It is also the Alpine town one must pass through to visit the Jungfrau, and together with First, Jungfrau, Wengen, Lauterbrunnen and others, it forms the most magnificent and beautiful scenic line in the Jungfrau region. In winter it is a famous snow-watching resort, and in summer it is a paradise for hiking enthusiasts. The lakeside, green grasslands, waterfalls, dairy cows and snow mountains together make this fascinating town.
attractions.text_zh: 位于艾格峰(Eiger)的北麓脚下的格林德尔瓦尔德(Grindelwald) ，这里有摄影家心头最爱的瑞士“梦幻山坡”著名明信片拍摄地，也是游览少女峰必经的阿尔卑斯小镇，同菲斯特(First)、少女峰(Jungfrau)、翁根(Wengen)、劳特布龙嫩（Lauterbrunnen）等一起组成了少女峰地区最为壮阔美丽的风景线。冬季是著名的观雪胜地，夏季又是徒步爱好者的天堂，湖畔、绿草青地、瀑布、奶牛、雪山共同构成让这座让人着迷的小镇。

attractions.text_en: Lauterbrunnen is located to the west of the Jungfrau region. More than 70 waterfalls and small rivers fall down from the cliffs, so the literal translation of Lauterbrunnen from German is "many springs," and this town is truly a town of waterfalls. Among them, the Staubbach Falls is the landmark scenery of the village, and the poets Goethe and Wordsworth wrote poems to praise it. In addition, the Mürren Falls and the Trümmelbach Falls, which rush down from caves as high as a ten-story building, should also not be missed. From Lauterbrunnen you can take the WAB railway to Wengen, Kleine Scheidegg, the Jungfrau and other places. At the same time, by taking the cable car via Mürren, you can reach the Schilthorn and other places, and feast your eyes on the magnificent scenery of the Bernese Highlands.
attractions.text_zh: 劳特布龙嫩(Lauterbrunnen)位于少女峰地区的西面，从山崖上落下来70多个瀑布和小河，所以劳特布龙嫩的德语直译是很多的泉水，这个小镇是名副其实的瀑布镇。其中施陶河瀑布是村子的标志性景观，诗人歌德和伍德·沃斯曾写诗来赞美它。此外还有米伦瀑布和从十层楼高的山洞里飞奔而下的特吕默尔河瀑布，也不可错过。从劳特布龙嫩出发可以乘WAB铁路到达翁根、克莱纳谢德格、少女峰等地。同时，乘坐缆车经米伦，可到达雪朗峰等地，饱览伯尔尼高地的雄伟风光。

attractions.text_en: Lake Brienz at the northern foot of the Alps, with its blue-green water known as the high-grade Tiffany blue, has the reputation of being "the left eye of God." Lake Brienz is the purest lake water in Switzerland. The book "Travel the World (Switzerland)" describes it like this: "A village full of charm and tranquility, with delightful street architecture, azure lake water, a mysterious lake, dense forests, lovely steam locomotives and a panorama of the Alps - all the various charms of Switzerland are concentrated here"......
attractions.text_zh: 阿尔卑斯山北麓的布里恩茨湖，湖水蓝中带绿，被称为Tiffany高级蓝的湖水，有着“上帝的左眼”的美誉。布里恩茨湖是瑞士最为纯净的湖水，《走遍全球（瑞士）》书上这么描述：“一个极富风情的恬静村子，富有情趣的街道建筑、蔚蓝色的湖水、神秘的湖泊、茂密的森林、可爱的蒸汽机车和阿尔卑斯全景图，瑞士的各种魅力在这里浓缩”......

attractions.text_en: Lausanne is the capital of the Canton of Vaud, located in the center of the northern shore of Lake Geneva, and it once flourished because it was a Roman military port. Through continuous development, this modern city of Lausanne has become one of the most important railway hubs in Switzerland. Most of the trains heading to major cities such as Geneva, Bern and Zurich converge here, and it is at the same time a transportation center connecting Paris and Milan. Lausanne is also called the Olympic Capital; both the International Olympic Committee and the Olympic Museum are located here.
attractions.text_zh: 洛桑是沃州的首府，位于日内瓦湖畔北部沿岸的中心，因曾是罗马的军港而繁荣。经过不断地发展，洛桑这座现代化的城市已经成为瑞士最重要的铁路枢纽之一。大多数通往日内瓦、伯尔尼、苏黎世等大城市的火车都在此交汇，它同时也是连接巴黎及米兰的交通中心。洛桑也被人们称为奥林匹克之都，国际奥林匹克委员会和奥林匹克博物馆均在此地。

attractions.text_en: Lausanne is the city designated by Baron de Coubertin, the father of the modern Olympics, as the home base of the International Olympic Committee (IOC). To carry on Coubertin's will to spread the Olympic spirit to all parts of the world, the world's first Olympic Museum was established in Lausanne in 1993, as designated by former IOC President Samaranch. The Lausanne Olympic Museum collects and displays tens of thousands of treasured items from the first Athens Olympics to the present, and it is the world's center of Olympic movement resources. The museum is located on the picturesque shore of Lake Geneva in an excellent environment, and from the verdant courtyard or the restaurant terrace one can gaze at the beautiful lake and mountain scenery of Lausanne.
attractions.text_zh: 洛桑是近代奥林匹克之父顾拜旦（Coubertin）男爵为国际奥林匹克委员会（IOC）根据地所指定的城市，为继承顾拜旦将奥林匹克的精神传播到世界各地的意志，由前任奥委会主席萨马兰奇指定，于1993年在洛桑建立了世界上一座奥林匹克博物馆。洛桑奥林匹克博物馆（Olympic Museum）收藏并展示了从第一届雅典奥运会至今的上万件珍藏品，是世界的奥林匹克运动资料中心。博物馆坐落在风光绰约的日内瓦湖畔，环境极佳，从葱郁的庭院或餐厅的阳台上可以眺望洛桑美丽的湖光山色。

attractions.text_en: The Lavaux Vineyard Terraces, inscribed on the World Cultural Heritage List, have a history of 800 years so far. Thanks to abundant sunshine and the humid climate by the lake, not only do the grapes produced here become very sweet, but the area has also become a famous wine-producing region of Switzerland. In the season when the grapes ripen, the air is filled with the aroma of wine.
attractions.text_zh: 被列入世界文化遗产名录的拉沃葡萄园梯田，迄今为止已有800年的历史。凭借充足的阳光和湖畔湿润的气候，不但让这里产出的葡萄非常甘甜，而且还使当地成为瑞士著名的葡萄酒产地。葡萄成熟的季节，空气里都弥漫着葡萄酒的香气。

attractions.text_en: Lake Geneva, also known as Lake Léman, straddles Switzerland and France. It is an important lake of Western Europe, and also a famous scenic area and health resort. During the day you can take a pleasure boat to tour Lake Geneva, and at night be intoxicated by the flickering neon lights along the shore, and there are often luxury cruise ships holding concerts or balls on the lake. You can take a pleasure boat to appreciate the beautiful scenery along the shore of Lake Geneva, watching the lake water, snow mountains and blue sky merge into one.
attractions.text_zh: 日内瓦湖又名莱芒湖，横跨瑞士和法国，是西欧的重要湖泊，也是著名的风景区和疗养地。白天你可以乘坐游船游览日内瓦湖，晚上陶醉于岸边闪烁的霓虹，还时常有豪华游轮在湖面上举办音乐会或舞会。可乘坐游船领略日内瓦湖岸边美景，看湖水、雪山和蓝天连成一片。

attractions.text_en: Chillon Castle is located at the foot of the Alps, on the shore of the charming Lake Geneva, and is a landmark medieval "floating on water" castle of Switzerland. The foundation stones of Chillon Castle are built on the lake bottom, giving people a feeling that it floats on the surface of the lake, yet the castle looks extraordinarily solid. Byron's "The Prisoner of Chillon," written with his pen, made this castle even more famous.
attractions.text_zh: 西庸城堡位于阿尔卑斯山脚下，充满魅力的日内瓦湖湖畔，是瑞士标志性的中世纪“水上漂”城堡。西庸城堡的基石建在湖底，给人一种漂浮在湖面上的感觉，但城堡看起来异常坚固，拜伦挥笔下的《西庸的囚徒》更是让这座城堡声名远扬。

attractions.text_en: Located in Dijon in the Burgundy region, the homeland of wine, Dijon was once the capital of the Duchy of Burgundy from the 11th to the 15th centuries. Especially in the 14th and 15th centuries, it flourished due to several outstanding leaders, becoming an important political, economic, cultural and artistic center of Europe at that time. Today, Dijon's old town is still scattered with many historical buildings from different periods. In addition to luxurious palaces, solemn churches, lavish glazed-tile roofs and many quaint timber-framed houses, there are also museums and art galleries converted from palaces and monasteries. Fine wine and fine food paired with an artistic atmosphere throughout the city make it a city with a very French character.
attractions.text_zh: 位于葡萄酒故乡勃垦第地区的第戎，11到-15世纪曾是勃垦第公国的首府，特别在14、15世纪时因几位优秀领导人而盛极一时，成为当时欧洲重要的政治经济和文化艺术中心。如今第戎的老城区仍散布着许多不同时期的历史建筑，除了豪华的宫殿、庄严的教堂、彩釉屋顶的豪华和许多古朴的木框架房屋，也有宫殿和修道院改装成的博物馆和美术馆。美酒、美食搭配满城艺术分为，是非常具有法国风情的一座城市。

attractions.text_en: Notre-Dame de Dijon was built in the 13th century. On the facade of the church, three rows of animal-shaped false gargoyles are separated by two rows of slender columns, and on the church there is a 14th-century jacquemart (striking clock). For most of the people of Dijon, Notre-Dame de Dijon is a church building with a sense of affinity, and over these several recent centuries, Notre-Dame has always been the main spiritual and emotional comfort of the people of Dijon.
attractions.text_zh: 第戎圣母院建造于13世纪，教堂门面上三列兽形假排水口由两排纤细的圆柱分开，教堂上有一座14世纪的报时钟。对于大多数的第戎人民而言，第戎圣母院是一座具有亲和力的教堂建筑，在这近几个世纪以来，圣母院一直是第戎人民主要的精神、心灵的抚慰。

attractions.text_en: This destination that combines winemaking and tourism in one is a place not to be missed by wine lovers, art lovers and food lovers. Choose a winery producing Burgundy products for a visit, where you can see the aging and maturing process of the wine and the storage and fermentation process in the cellar, and if you are lucky you can see the winemaking process of the wine (depending on the grape season).
attractions.text_zh: 这个集酿酒与旅游于一身的胜地是葡萄酒爱好者、艺术爱好者和美食爱好者所不容错过的地方。选择勃艮第产品的酒庄进行参观，在此可参观葡萄酒的陈酿熟化过程及酒窖内储存发酵过程，运气好的时候可参观葡萄酒的酿造过程（取决葡萄的产期）。

attractions.text_en: If the Louvre bears witness to the glory of the Gauls, then the Île de la Cité, Notre-Dame de Paris, and the Hôtel de Ville write the inseparable bond with the Romans who conquered them. Without the Romans, there would be no Île de la Cité. Without the Île de la Cité, there would be no Paris as it is today.
attractions.text_zh: 如果说卢浮宫见证了高卢人的荣耀，那西岱岛，巴黎圣母院和巴黎市政厅则写下了与征服她们的罗马人的不解之缘。如果没有罗马人，就没有西岱岛。没有西岱岛，就没有现在的巴黎。

attractions.text_en: The Cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris stands on the Île de la Cité in the heart of Paris and is the cathedral of the Catholic Archdiocese of Paris. The church is Gothic in style, and visitors can climb the towers. Although you won't meet the "Hunchback of Notre-Dame," you can see the 17th-century great bell, get up close to the mythical beasts on the church's exterior walls, and overlook the beautiful scenery of the Île de la Cité and the Seine.
attractions.text_zh: 巴黎圣母院大教堂坐落于巴黎市中心的西岱岛上，是天主教巴黎总教区的主教座堂。教堂为哥特式风格，游客可以登上塔楼参观，虽然遇不到“钟楼怪人”，但却能看到17世纪的大钟、近距离接触教堂外壁上的神兽、俯瞰西岱岛与塞纳河的美丽风光。

attractions.text_en:
The Champ de Mars is a large ribbon-shaped park located in the 7th arrondissement of Paris, France, lying between the Eiffel Tower to its northwest and the École Militaire (Military School) to its southeast.
Before the second half of the 18th century, this was an abandoned plot of land. It was not until the Military School was established in 1765 that its status gradually rose, when it was mainly used as a place for military training. On 17 July 1791, during the French Revolution, a bloody massacre took place here. Later, France used this site to host several World's Fairs as well as the Olympic Games of 1900.
attractions.text_zh:
战神广场是一个座落于法国巴黎七区的广大带状公园，介于位在其西北方的埃菲尔铁塔、以及在其东南方的巴黎军校之间。
在十八世纪下半叶之前，这是一块被遗弃的空地，直到军事学校在1765年建立，它的地位才逐渐提升，当时主要的目的是用来作为军事训练的场所。在法国大革命时期的1791年7月17日，此处曾发生屠杀流血事件。后来法国曾经使用这个场地迎接过几次的世界博览会以及1900年的奥林匹克运动会。

attractions.text_en: The Eiffel Tower is one of the symbols of Paris, affectionately called the "Iron Lady" by the French. If Notre-Dame de Paris is the symbol of ancient Paris, then the Eiffel Tower is the emblem of modern Paris.
attractions.text_zh: 埃菲尔铁塔是巴黎的标志之一，被法国人爱称为“铁娘子”。如果说，巴黎圣母院是古代巴黎的象征，那么埃菲尔铁塔就是现代巴黎的标志。

attractions.text_en: Take the famous "Bateaux Mouches" cruise with its transparent cabins, sailing along the banks of the Seine that bring together many attractions. The 3.2-kilometer, roughly 1-hour voyage passes Notre-Dame de Paris, the Grand and Petit Palais, the Pont Alexandre III, the Paris love-lock bridge, the Louvre, the Champs-Élysées, and the Île de la Cité, among other famous sights. On the surface of the Seine, you can experience Paris's romantic night views, beautiful scenery, and wonderful music. It is hailed as "the in-depth touring experience in Paris not to be missed."
attractions.text_zh: 乘坐着名的透明船舱的“苍蝇船”游船航行在融合诸多景点的塞纳河畔，3.2公里约1小时的航程途径巴黎圣母院，大小皇宫、亚历山大三世大桥，巴黎锁桥，卢浮宫、香榭丽舍大道巴黎西岱岛等著名景点，在塞纳河面上感受巴黎浪漫夜色，美景及美妙的音乐，被称为《巴黎不可错过的深度游览项目》。

attractions.text_en: It is one of the world's oldest museums and a world-renowned treasure house of art. The Louvre building is "U"-shaped, divided into two parts, new and old. The glass pyramid entrance in front of the Louvre was designed by the Chinese-American master architect I. M. Pei. Here are preserved more than 400,000 art treasures, of which about 35,000 are on long-term display, encompassing works from ancient Egypt, Greece, Etruria, and Rome to medieval Europe and the countries of the East — truly an outstanding palace of art in human history. The itinerary includes a standard individual-visitor ticket (no Chinese audio guide).
attractions.text_zh: 是世界上古老的博物馆之一，是一座举世闻名的艺术宝库。卢浮宫建筑呈“U”形，分别为新、旧两个部分。卢浮宫前玻璃金字塔的入口是华裔建筑大师贝聿铭所设计。在这里珍藏着40余万件艺术珍品、其中长期展出约3.5万件，囊括了古埃及、希腊、伊特鲁里亚、罗马到欧洲中世纪和东方各国的艺术品，堪称人类历史上杰出的艺术殿堂。 行程包含普通散客门票（无中文讲解器）。

attractions.text_en: The Palace of Versailles has always been a magnificent monument of the era of France's feudal rule. In terms of its content, it was not only the French court but also the administrative center of the state, and a concrete embodiment of the social, political views and lifestyle of France at the time. It represented the power of an absolutist regime able to concentrate such enormous manpower, material resources, and finances on a scale unseen in Europe since the ancient Roman Empire. At that time, in order to build it, Louis XIV mobilized more than thirty thousand workers as well as architects, engineers, and technicians. Besides solving the complex technical problems arising from constructing such a large-scale building complex, they also had to resolve various issues such as water supply and roads. Clearly, the success of the Palace of Versailles powerfully demonstrates the economic and technological progress of France at that time and the wisdom of its working people.
attractions.text_zh: 凡尔赛宫始终是法国封建统治历史时期的一座华丽的纪念碑。从内容上讲不仅是法兰西宫廷，而且是国家的行政中心，也是当时法国社会政治观点、生活方式的具体体现。它是欧洲自古罗马帝国以来表现出能够集中如此巨大的人力、物力、财力的专制政体力量。当时，路易十四为了建造它，共动用了三万余名工人和建筑师、工程师、技师，除了要解决建造大规模建筑群所产生的复杂技术问题外，还要解决引水、道路等各方面的问题。可见，凡尔赛宫的成功，有力地证明了当时法国经济和技术的进步和劳动人民的智慧。

attractions.text_en: Boulevard Haussmann lies between the 8th and 9th arrondissements of Paris. On the street are the two great department stores, Galeries Lafayette and Printemps. Marcel Proust, author of In Search of Lost Time, completed most of the writing of In Search of Lost Time in his residence at number 102 on this street.
attractions.text_zh: 奥斯曼大街位于巴黎第八区和第九区之间。街上有巴黎老佛爷百货公司和春天百货两大百货公司。《追忆似水年华》的作者马塞尔·普鲁斯特曾在这条街102号的居所里，完成了《追忆似水年华》的大部分写作。

attractions.text_en: The Paris Opera was designed by Charles Garnier in 1861. Its architecture perfectly combines several architectural forms, including the ancient Greco-Roman colonnade and the Baroque. Grand in scale, exquisite and refined, resplendent and magnificent, it is hailed as a theater where painting, marble, and gold ornamentation shine upon one another, offering great enjoyment. It is one of the typical buildings of Napoleon III's era.
attractions.text_zh: 巴黎歌剧院是由查尔斯·加尼叶于1861年设计的，其建筑将古希腊罗马式柱廊、巴洛克等几种建筑形式完美地结合在一起，规模宏大，精美细致，金碧辉煌，被誉为是一座绘画、大理石和金饰交相辉映的剧院，给人以极大的享受。是拿破仑三世典型的建筑之一。

attractions.text_en: Mont Saint-Michel is located on a solitary island on the Normandy coast, two kilometers from the shore. With its remarkable phenomenon of becoming a sea island at high tide and joining the mainland at low tide, it has been listed by UNESCO as a World Heritage of mankind. Mont Saint-Michel is a famous French monument and a Christian holy site, situated on a small island in the Manche department, two kilometers from the coast. The island is cone-shaped, with a circumference of 900 meters, formed of towering granite. At an elevation of 88 meters, it is often surrounded by vast stretches of sandbank and only becomes an island at high tide. In ancient times this was a place where the Celts worshipped their gods. In the 8th century AD, the cardinal Aubert built a small chapel-castle on the highest point of the island, dedicated to the Archangel Michael, which has now become a center of pilgrimage, hence the name Mont Saint-Michel. It is a "World Cultural and Natural Heritage" site.
attractions.text_zh: 圣米歇尔山位于诺曼底海滩上的一座孤岛上，距海岸两公里，涨潮时为海岛，退潮时为陆地的奇特景观，被联合国教科文组列入世界人类遗产。圣米歇尔山是法国著名古迹和基督教圣地，位于芒什省一小岛上，距海岸两公里。小岛呈圆锥形，周长900米，由耸立的花岗石构成。海拔88米，经常被大片沙岸包围，仅涨潮时才成为岛。古时这里是凯尔特人祭神的地方。公元8世纪，红衣主教奥贝在岛上最高处修建一座小教堂城堡，奉献给天使长米歇尔，如今成为朝圣中心，故称圣米歇尔山，是“世界文化与自然遗产”景点。

attractions.text_en: The Abbey of Mont Saint-Michel is located on Mont Saint-Michel. Shaped by nature, Mont Saint-Michel is one of the world's great wonders, and the abbey and great church atop it hold a supreme place in the hearts of Christians. Aubert built a small chapel on the island, dedicated to the Archangel Michael, and it became a center of pilgrimage. During Napoleon's rule, the abbey also served as a state prison. To this day, although the abbey has been designed and rebuilt by many architects, it still retains a plain, unadorned, and antique style.
attractions.text_zh: 圣米歇尔山修道院位于圣米歇尔山上。圣米歇尔山经大自然的造化，是世界的一大奇观，山上的修道院和大教堂更在基督教徒的心目中有着至高无上的地位。奥伯特在岛上修建一座小教堂，奉献给大天使米歇尔，成为朝圣中心。拿破仑执政期间，修道院也曾作为国家监狱。到如今，修道院虽然经诸多建筑师设计改建，但依旧保持着朴实无华、古色古香的格调。

attractions.text_en: The largest of the châteaux of the Loire Valley, with a fairly typical medieval castle layout, it was a hunting lodge for the kings of France. Château de Chambord was built during the reign of Francis I. In its layout, form, and decorative style, it reflects France's traditional architectural art, while also being influenced by the Italian Renaissance, making it one of the representative works of the French Renaissance period, regarded by the French as a national treasure worth boasting of. In 1981, Château de Chambord was inscribed on the World Heritage List.
attractions.text_zh: 卢瓦尔河谷城堡群中最大的一个，较为典型的中世纪古堡布局，是法国君王狩猎的行宫。香波堡兴建于弗朗斯瓦一世时期。它在布局、造型、风格装饰上反映了法国传统的建筑艺术，同时又受到意大利文艺复兴的影响，成为法国文艺复兴时期的代表作之一，被法国人视为值得炫耀的国宝。1981年，香博堡列入了世界遗产名录。

attractions.text_en: The Old Town of Rennes is the most charming district of this capital of Brittany, preserving a large number of 16th–17th century half-timbered houses — colorful and with leaning lines, full of medieval flavor. Strolling among them, the stone streets, arched gateways, and ancient squares interweave into a rich historical atmosphere. This is not only the historical and cultural center but also the most lively part of Rennes; cafés, restaurants, and specialty shops abound, and the university-town atmosphere fills the old district with youthful energy. Whether for in-depth cultural touring, architectural photography, or a slow-paced stroll, the Old Town of Rennes is well worth savoring in detail.
attractions.text_zh: 雷恩老城是这座布列塔尼首府最具魅力的区域，保存着大量16–17世纪的木筋房（半木结构建筑），色彩斑斓、线条倾斜，极具中世纪风情。漫步其间，石板街道、拱形门洞与古老广场交织成浓厚的历史氛围。这里不仅是历史文化中心，也是雷恩最具生活气息的地方，咖啡馆、餐厅、特色小店林立，大学城氛围让古老街区充满年轻活力。无论是文化深度游、建筑摄影还是慢节奏漫步，雷恩老城都值得细细品味。

attractions.text_en: Parc du Thabor is one of the most famous and most worth-visiting urban green spaces in Rennes, covering about 10 hectares. It combines a French geometric garden, an English-style natural landscape area, a vast rose garden, and a botanical garden, suitable for all four seasons, and is a popular spot for locals and tourists to relax and stroll. The park originally developed from the garden of a Benedictine abbey, and was later redesigned in the 19th century by the renowned landscape designer Denis Bühler, forming the refined yet natural landscape structure it has today. Here there are more than 3,000 species of plants and over 2,000 varieties of roses, as well as a music pavilion, an aviary, fountains, and a children's play area, and it regularly hosts concerts, festival events, and cultural exhibitions — a place not to be missed for experiencing the natural and cultural life of Rennes. The best times to stroll are during the spring and summer flowering season and the autumn maple-leaf season; it is also suitable for families, couples, or photography enthusiasts to visit.
attractions.text_zh: 他泊花园是雷恩著名且最值得一访的城市绿地之一，面积约 10 公顷，融合了法式几何庭园、英式自然风景区、庞大的玫瑰园与植物园，四季皆宜，是当地人和游客休闲散步的热门场所。公园最初由本笃会修道院的庭园发展而来，后于19世纪由著名景观设计师 Denis Bühler 重新布局，形成如今既精致又自然的景观结构。这里拥有超过 3000种植物 和 2000余种玫瑰，还设有 音乐亭、鸟舍、喷泉与儿童游乐区，常年举办音乐会、节庆活动和文化展览，是体验雷恩自然与文化生活不可错过的地点。最佳漫步时间为春夏花季及秋日枫叶时节，同时也适合家庭、情侣或摄影爱好者前来探访。

attractions.text_en: The Palace of the Parliament of Brittany (exterior) is one of the most representative historical landmarks of Rennes. Built in the 17th century, it is a model work of French classical architecture. This was once the seat of the judicial institution of the Brittany region, witnessing the region's important status in French history. The building's exterior is dignified and elegant, while its interior decoration is sumptuous, especially famous for its ceiling murals and golden reliefs, displaying the combination of royal power and local tradition. Today it still functions as a court of law, but it is open for guided tours at certain times, and visitors can gain an in-depth understanding of Brittany's political, legal, and cultural history through the commentary. As one of the core attractions of the Old Town of Rennes, the Parliament Palace is surrounded by squares, cafés, and medieval streets and lanes, a symbol of the convergence of the city's history and modern life, and an important stop not to be missed on an in-depth cultural tour.
attractions.text_zh: 布列塔尼议会宫（ 外观 ）是雷恩最具代表性的历史地标之一，建于17世纪，是法国古典主义建筑的典范之作。这里曾是布列塔尼地区司法机构所在地，见证了该地区在法国历史中的重要地位。建筑外观庄重典雅，内部装饰华丽，尤其以天花板壁画和金色浮雕闻名，展现了王权与地方传统的结合。如今这里仍作为法院使用，但在特定时段开放导览参观，游客可通过讲解深入了解布列塔尼的政治、法律与文化历史。作为雷恩老城核心景点之一，议会宫周边环绕着广场、咖啡馆和中世纪街巷，是城市历史与现代生活交汇的象征，也是文化深度游不可错过的重要一站。

attractions.text_en: The magnificent Colosseum, located in the center of Rome, was built by the ancient Romans to entertain the generals and soldiers returning in triumph and to glorify the great Roman Empire. It was the place where slave owners, nobles and free citizens of the ancient Roman Empire watched beast fights or gladiatorial combat between slaves, and it is one of Rome's more famous historical relics. The Colosseum is a masterpiece and miracle of architectural history, renowned throughout the world for being huge, majestic and magnificent. Although only the greater part of its skeleton remains, its grand bearing and imposing momentum still endure.
attractions.text_zh: 位于罗马中心的壮观的斗兽场是古罗马当时为取悦凯旋而归的将领士兵和赞美伟大的古罗马帝国而建造的，是古罗马帝国专供奴隶主、贵族和自由民观看斗兽或奴隶角斗的地方,是罗马较著名的历史遗迹。斗兽场在建筑史上堪称典范的杰作和奇迹，以庞大、雄伟、壮观著称于世。虽只剩下大半个骨架，但其雄伟之气魄、磅礴之气势犹存。

attractions.text_en: The Pantheon is a well-preserved building of ancient Rome, with a history of more than 2,000 years. The artist Raphael, the Roman king Vittorio Emanuele II and Umberto I, among others, rest here. The Pantheon was built by Agrippa, son-in-law of Octavian, the first emperor of the Roman Empire, to worship the gods of Mount Olympus. It can be called a classic building of the Augustan period, a model of the exquisite building techniques of ancient Rome, and was praised by Michelangelo as "the design of angels".
attractions.text_zh: 万神殿是古罗马保存完好的建筑，距今已有2000多年的历史，艺术家拉斐尔、罗马皇帝维托里奥﹒埃曼努埃尔二世和温布尔一世等长眠于此。万神殿由罗马帝国首任皇帝屋大维的女婿阿戈利巴建造，用以供奉奥林匹亚山上诸神，可谓奥古斯都时期的经典建筑，是古罗马精湛建筑技术的典范，被米开朗基罗赞叹为"天使的设计"。

attractions.text_en: The Spanish Square once appeared in scenes of the film "Roman Holiday", becoming a calling card of Rome that everyone knows. Audrey Hepburn's fresh and beautiful figure seems to have merged with the Spanish Square and beautiful Rome, becoming a memory that people can never shake off. The Spanish Square itself is not large, and the crowds are mainly concentrated near the Spanish Steps. The steps have 138 levels in total, the widest steps in Europe.
attractions.text_zh: 西班牙广场曾经出现在电影《罗马假日》的场景中，成为了所有人都知道的罗马名片。赫本那清新亮丽的倩影似乎已经与西班牙广场以及美丽的罗马合为一体，成为了人们永远挥之不去的记忆。西班牙台阶整个西班牙广场面积不大，人群主要集中在西班牙台阶附近。整个台阶共138级，是欧洲最宽的台阶。

attractions.name_zh: 特莱维喷泉(许愿池)（外观）
attractions.text_en: Also known as the "Fountain of the Maiden" or the "Wishing Pool", it takes as its theme the return of the sea god Neptune in victory from Roman mythology, with a magnificent and grand momentum. It is said that if you stand with your back to the fountain and toss a coin over your shoulder, and it lands in the water, your dream will come true.
attractions.text_zh: 又称"少女喷泉"，或者"许愿池"，以罗马神话中海神尼普勒战胜归来为题材，整气势磅礴、大气恢宏。据说背对着喷泉、从肩上投出一枚硬币，如果能投进水中，就能梦想成真。

attractions.text_en: The winding cypress lane, a country road lined with Italian cypresses on both sides, with its uniquely shaped curves and neatly arranged cypress trees, has become one of the symbols of the pastoral scenery of Tuscany. The cypresses on both sides of the road cast mottled shadows under the sunlight, creating a tranquil and poetic atmosphere that attracts countless photography enthusiasts and tourists to come and take photos.
attractions.text_zh: 蜿蜒曲折丝柏树小径、两旁种满意大利柏树的乡村道路，独特造型的弯道和整齐排列的丝柏树，成为托斯卡纳田园风光的象征之一。道路两侧的柏树在阳光的照射下投下斑驳的影子，营造出一种宁静而诗意的氛围，吸引了无数摄影爱好者和游客前来打卡。

attractions.text_en: Pienza, a small town located on a hill in the Val d'Orcia in southern Tuscany, is the birthplace of Pope Pius II. In 1459, Pienza, which he presided over rebuilding, was regarded as a model of the "ideal city", and was therefore hailed as the "touchstone of Renaissance urban life". Unlike other Italian medieval towns, the unique feature of Pienza is that one can look out from the city walls over the scenery of the World Heritage site - the "Val d'Orcia".
attractions.text_zh: 皮恩扎，位于托斯卡尼南部奥尔恰山谷山丘上的小镇，这里是教皇皮乌斯二世的出生地，1459年他主持重建的皮恩扎被视为"理想城市"的典范，因此被誉为"文艺复兴都市生活的试金石"。与其他意大利中世纪小镇不同，皮恩扎独特之处在于可以从城墙上俯瞰世界遗产-「Val D'orcia奥尔恰山谷」的景致。

attractions.text_en: Siena, this medieval ancient city, is full of masterpieces by great artists and medieval classical architecture. Wandering through Siena's ancient, crisscrossing stone-paved alleys and the deep courtyards hidden in various places, you seem to be transported back to medieval Europe a thousand years ago, and every detail will bring you surprises. For several centuries, this city has maintained its complete classical appearance, as if time stopped here in the fourteenth century. In addition to its unique scenery and art collections, Siena also maintains a series of traditions, including the cooking and making of desserts. On your trip to Siena, don't forget to taste the local specialties - almond soft cakes ricciarelli, nut tart panforte, peppered spiced nut cake pan pepato, rice pudding budini di riso, walnut cakes cavallucci, or walnut wafers copate!
attractions.text_zh: 锡耶纳，这座中世纪古城布满了艺术大师的杰作和中世纪古典建筑，穿梭在锡耶纳古老而纵横交错的石板小巷和隐藏在各处的幽深庭院中，您仿佛置身于千年前的欧洲中世纪，其中的每一处细节都会给您带来惊喜。这座城市在几个世纪以来，一直保持着完整的古典外貌，仿佛时间自十四世纪起便在此停滞。除了它独特的风景和艺术典藏，锡耶纳还保持着一系列的传统，包括甜品的烹饪制作。在您的锡耶纳之行中，别忘了品尝当地的特色- 杏仁软饼ricciarelli、果仁馅饼panforte、胡椒香料果仁糕pan pepato、米饭布丁budini di riso、核桃饼cavallucci、或核桃华夫copate!

attractions.text_en: Piazza del Campo (also called the Shell Square because it resembles a shell) is the center of the city of Siena, and one of the larger surviving medieval squares in Europe. Directly opposite the square are the Siena Town Hall and the tower. Inside the Town Hall is the Civic Museum, which houses numerous paintings of the Sienese School, including Ambrogio Lorenzetti's "Allegory of Good Government" and "Allegory of Bad Government". The tower's full name is Torre del Mangia. It is the taller building in Siena and the second-tallest tower in Italy, with a vertical height of 102 meters. Visitors can reach the top via 55 flights of stairs and take in the beautiful views of the city of Siena.
attractions.text_zh: 田野广场(因形似贝壳也叫贝壳广场)是锡耶纳城市的中心，是欧洲现存较大的中世纪广场之一，广场正对面是锡耶纳市政厅和塔楼，市政厅内有市立博物馆，里面藏有锡耶纳学派的众多绘画作品，包括AmbrogioLorenzetti的《好政府寓意》和《坏政府寓意》。塔楼全名为曼贾塔TorrediMangia，它是锡耶纳较高的建筑，也是意大利第二高的塔楼，垂直高度达到102米，游客可以通过55层的阶梯达到顶端，一览锡耶纳城的美景。

attractions.text_en: The Val d'Orcia was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2004. Tuscany's Val d'Orcia is famous for its postcard-perfect scenery and hill towns. It is a unique convergence of art and nature, holding rich historical traces. Its natural landscape was redesigned during the Renaissance, embodying the concept of "good government" of the Italian city-states.
attractions.text_zh: 奥尔恰山谷，2004年被列入联合国教科文组织世界遗产名录。托斯卡纳的 Val d'Orcia 以其明信片般的完美风景和山城而闻名，这里是艺术与自然的独特交汇，蕴藏着丰富的历史印记，其自然景观在文艺复兴时期经过重新设计，体现了意大利城邦"良政"的理念。

attractions.text_en: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is the freestanding bell tower of the cathedral of the Italian city of Pisa. Located on the Square of Miracles on the north side of the city of Pisa in the province of Tuscany, Italy, it is a world-famous landmark and one of the symbols of Italy, and was selected as a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. It is said that in 1590 AD, Galileo conducted a free-fall experiment on the Leaning Tower of Pisa and overturned the views of Aristotle.
attractions.text_zh: 比萨斜塔是意大利比萨城大教堂的独立式钟楼，位于意大利托斯卡纳省比萨城北面的奇迹广场上，是世界著名的景观，也是意大利标志之一，被联合国教育科学文化组织评选为世界遗产。相传在公元1590年，伽利略曾经在比萨斜塔上做了一个自由落体实验，并推翻了亚里士多德的观点。

attractions.text_en: Also known as the Cathedral Square, it is a walled area in the center of the city of Pisa in the Tuscany region of Italy, part of which is grassland. The Square of Miracles is the center of the city of Pisa, with four major buildings on the square: the Pisa Cathedral, the Leaning Tower of Pisa (bell tower), the Baptistery of St. John, and the Pisa Cemetery. Around the square, the ancient city walls and city gates are also preserved. In April 1987, the International Council on Monuments and Sites nominated the Square of Miracles of the Italian city of Pisa (including the cathedral, the baptistery, the Leaning Tower of Pisa and the cemetery) as a World Heritage Site.
attractions.text_zh: 又名主教座堂广场，是意大利托斯卡纳大区比萨市中心一片围墙包围的区域，部分为草地。奇迹广场是比萨城的中心，广场上有四大建筑：比萨主教座堂、比萨斜塔（钟楼）、圣乔瓦尼洗礼堂、比萨墓园。广场周围还保留着古代的城墙和城门。1987年4月，国际古迹遗产理事会提名意大利比萨城的奇迹广场（包括大教堂、洗礼堂、比萨斜塔和墓园）为世界遗产。

attractions.text_en: The Cinque Terre is located in the coastal area of the province of La Spezia in the Liguria region of Italy. Built along the winding Mediterranean coastline, it is the collective name for five clifftop villages: Monterosso al Mare, Vernazza, Corniglia, Manarola and Riomaggiore. What is special is the hiking trail that strings the five villages together; built along the coast, it is one of the world's beautiful hiking routes, about 15-20 kilometers, taking about 5 hours. On one side is the Mediterranean, on the other side are the mountains, and ahead are the Italian fishing villages, making it a destination that many hiking enthusiasts flock to. In 1997 it was inscribed on the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage List.
attractions.text_zh: 五渔村位于意大利利古里亚大区(Liguria)拉斯佩齐亚省海沿岸地区，沿着蜿蜒的地中海海岸线而建，是蒙特罗索（Monterosso al Mare）、韦尔纳扎（Vernazza）、科尔尼利亚（Corniglia）、马纳罗拉（Manarola）及里奥马焦雷（Riomaggiore）这五个悬崖边上的村镇的统称。特别的是把五个村庄串起的徒步线路，沿着海岸而建，是世界上美丽的徒步路线，约15-20公里，需5小时左右，一边是地中海，另一边是山脉，而前面则是意大利渔庄，是诸多徒步爱好者趋之若鹜的胜地。1997年被联合国教科文组织列入世界文化遗产名录。

attractions.name_en: Florence Cathedral (Santa Maria del Fiore) (queuing required to enter)
attractions.name_zh: 圣母百花大教堂（需排队进入）
attractions.text_en: There are many solemn and majestic churches in the world, but few can be so charming. This beautiful church, faced with white, red and green granite, interprets the classical, elegant and free style admired in the Renaissance era to the fullest, so it is no wonder it was named "Saint Mary of the Flower". The original site of Florence Cathedral was the Church of Santa Reparata, built in the 4th century. In 1296, Giovanni de' Medici funded the building of the new church, which took 175 years to complete.
attractions.text_zh: 世界上庄严雄伟的教堂很多，但很少有教堂能如此妩媚。这座使用白、红、绿三色花岗岩贴面的美丽教堂将文艺复兴时代所推崇的古典、优雅、自由诠释得淋漓尽致，难怪会被命名为"花之圣母"。圣母百花大教堂原址是建于4世纪的圣·雷帕拉塔教堂。1296年，乔凡尼·美第奇出资建造新的教堂，花了175年时间才建成。

attractions.text_en: Piazza della Signoria enjoys the reputation of being an open-air sculpture museum, and has also been the center of the city's political life for several centuries. On the square stand the Palazzo Vecchio (Old Palace), a replica of Michelangelo's sculpture "David", and the Fountain of Neptune. The open Piazza della Signoria is surrounded by the Uffizi Gallery, the Loggia dei Lanzi, and numerous cafes and bars. The famous Florence Cathedral (Santa Maria del Fiore) is located directly to its north.
attractions.text_zh: 市政广场有露天雕塑博物馆的美誉，也是该城数个世纪以来政治生活中心，广场上坐落着维琪奥宫（旧宫）、米开朗基罗雕塑作品《大卫》的复制品以及海神喷泉。开放式的市政广场，周围环绕着乌菲兹美术馆、佣兵凉亭和众多的咖啡厅、酒吧。著名的圣母百花大教堂就位于它的正北方。

attractions.text_en: Located on the Arno River, the Ponte Vecchio is also called the Old Bridge. Built in 1345, it is one of the earlier large-span circular-arch bridges in Europe. It is said that Dante encountered the maiden Beatrice here, and later poured his feelings of longing into "The Divine Comedy". Over the centuries, it has withstood floods, social upheavals and wars while remaining standing. It is one of the famous landmark buildings of Florence.
attractions.text_zh: 位于阿诺河(Arno)上的维奇奥桥，又被称为老桥。建于1345年，是欧洲较早的大跨度圆弧拱桥，据说但丁在这里邂逅了少女比阿特丽丝，后来将思慕之情倾注到《神曲》之中。几百年间，它经历过的水灾、社会动乱、战争而屹立不倒。是佛罗伦萨的著名地标建筑之一。

attractions.text_en: Piazza Santa Croce is located in the center of Florence, Italy, and is an important public space that has continued from the Renaissance to the present. The core of the square is the magnificent Basilica of Santa Croce. This Gothic building is the resting place of Italian celebrities such as Michelangelo and Galileo, and the frescoes and sculptures inside are worth savoring in detail. There are several cafes and restaurants around the square, offering authentic Italian cuisine, making it an ideal place to rest and taste local flavors. Piazza Santa Croce is also a venue for various cultural activities and festivals; especially in summer, open-air concerts and theatrical performances attract many spectators. The open space of the square is suitable for strolling and admiring the surrounding architecture, experiencing the unique atmosphere of Florence.
attractions.text_zh: 圣十字广场位于意大利佛罗伦萨市中心，是文艺复兴时期延续至今的重要公共空间，广场的核心是宏伟的圣十字大教堂这座哥特式建筑是米开朗基罗和伽利略等意大利名人的安息地，内部的壁画和雕塑值得细细品味。广场周围有多家咖啡馆和餐厅，提供地道的意大利美食，是休憩和品尝当地风味的理想之地。圣十字广场也是举办各种文化活动和节庆的场所，尤其在夏季，露天音乐会和戏剧表演吸引众多观众。广场的开放空间适合漫步和观赏周围的建筑，感受佛罗伦萨的独特氛围。

attractions.text_en: Known for short as St. Mark's Basilica, it stands on St. Mark's Square in the center of Venice. First built in 829 AD and rebuilt from 1043 to 1071, it was once the largest church in medieval Europe. It is a classic work of Venetian architectural art, and at the same time a treasury rich in artworks.
attractions.text_zh: 简称圣马可大教堂，是矗立于威尼斯市中心的圣马可广场上。始建于公元829年，重建于1043~1071年，它曾是中世纪欧洲大的教堂，是威尼斯建筑艺术的经典之作，它同时也是一座收藏丰富艺术品的宝库。

attractions.text_en: This is a museum located on Piazza San Marco, where all of Venice's past glory resides. Inside, you can see not only frescoes and the like, but also medieval weapons. When going to the dungeon, you walk across the Bridge of Sighs, and you can look outside through small holes, rather than having so many people on the bridge opposite looking over at you. You can take photos inside, as long as you don't use a flash.
attractions.text_zh: 这是一家位于Piazza San Marco的博物馆，威尼斯过去的辉煌都在这里了。里面可以看的不仅有壁画等，还有中世纪的兵器，去地牢的时候走过叹息桥，能通过小孔看外面，而不是像对面桥上面那么多的人在往你这里看叹息桥。里面是可以拍照片的，只要你不要用闪光灯就可以了。

attractions.text_en: The Bridge of Sighs was in ancient times the only route by which condemned prisoners were escorted from the court to the prison. The Bridge of Sighs is in early Baroque style. The bridge is shaped like a house, with a vaulted top, and is very tightly sealed; there are only two small windows on the side facing the canal. After a prisoner was tried at the Doge's Palace, the felon would be taken to the dungeon. When passing over this airtight bridge, he could only look at the blue sky through the small window, and from then on lost his freedom, involuntarily letting out sounds of sighing. It is said that if lovers kiss under the bridge, they can be together forever, and the film "A Little Romance" was filmed here.
attractions.text_zh: 叹息桥是古代由法院向监狱押送死囚的必经之路。叹息桥造型属早期巴洛克式风格，桥呈房屋状，上部穹隆覆盖，封闭得很严实，只有向运河一侧有两个小窗，当犯人在总督府接受审判之后，重罪犯被带到地牢中，在经过这座密不透气的的桥时，只能透过小窗看看蓝天，从此失去了自由，不自主的发出叹息之声。听说恋人们在桥下接吻就可以天长地久，而电影「情定日落桥」就在这取景的。

attractions.text_en: It is the central square of Venice, once called by Napoleon "the most beautiful drawing room of Europe". The square is surrounded on three sides by magnificent palaces, and to the east stand the tall bell tower and St. Mark's Basilica. Since ancient times, it has been the center of public activity for Venetian politics, religion and traditional festivals.
attractions.text_zh: 是威尼斯的中心广场，曾被拿破仑称作"欧洲美丽的客厅"。广场三面被宏伟壮丽的宫殿围绕，东面耸立着高大的钟楼和圣马可教堂。自古以来，就是威尼斯政治、宗教及传统节日的公共活动中心。

attractions.text_en: Although its name Duomo means "dome", Milan Cathedral is actually a Gothic building. Dominating the city center, Milan Cathedral was first built in 1386 and took 500 years to complete. Hailed as a magnificent Gothic building of Italy, hundreds of spires soar into the clouds, and thousands of statues are carved on the white marble exterior walls. The interior space is broad and spacious, with sunlight streaming in through the beautiful stained-glass mosaic paintings on the windows, the light dim, full of a solemn religious atmosphere. In the church's crypt, the remains of the Church of St. Tecla on the original site are also preserved.
attractions.text_zh: 虽然它的名字Duomo是"圆顶"的意思，米兰大教堂实际上却是一座哥特式建筑。雄踞在市中心的米兰大教堂始建于1386年，历经500年才完工，被誉为意大利壮丽的哥特式建筑数以百计的尖塔耸入云霄，白色大理石外墙上雕刻着数千座雕像。内部空间宽广，阳光透过窗上美丽的彩色玻璃镶嵌画射入，光线朦胧，充满庄严的宗教气氛。教堂地下室中还保留着原址上的圣·特库拉教堂的遗迹。

attractions.text_en: This is a gorgeous shopping arcade, but most tourists who come here are sightseeing. Under the central arcade there is a Prada boutique, whose scale and window displays are among the best in Milan, especially the window displays, which are updated daily; just standing outside the windows to admire them is a great pleasure. The Galleria Vittorio Emanuele is dotted with quite a few high-end restaurants, cafes and shops.
attractions.text_zh: 这是一个华丽的购物商场，不过多数来这里的游客都是来观光的。中央拱廊下有着Prada精品店，规模和橱窗布置都是米兰数一数二的，尤其橱窗布置天天更新，光是站在橱窗外欣赏也是一大乐事。埃马努埃莱长廊遍布了不少高级的餐馆、咖啡厅以及商店。

attractions.text_en: Bahnhofstrasse (also called Station Street) is one of the longer shopping avenues in Europe, and also one of the most expensive shopping avenues in the world. More precisely, it is not only the truly priceless center of Zurich, where luxury brands from all over the world are spread out and private banks stand in rows. Walking along Zurich's Bahnhofstrasse, countless visitors will feel an inexplicable excitement, because beneath their feet are stored gold bars whose quantity is said to be among the world's foremost, leaving many people too excited to move their feet. Immersed in it, you can clearly feel the air filled with the aura of wealth, luxury and leisure.
attractions.text_zh: 班霍夫大街(又称车站大街)是欧洲较长的购物大道，也是世界上昂贵的购物大道之一，更确切的说它不仅是苏黎世真正的寸土寸金的中心，这里遍布世界各地的奢侈品牌，私人银行林立。走在苏黎世的班霍夫大街上，无数游人都会产生莫名的兴奋，因为在他们脚下储藏着数量号称世界前列的金条，让很多人都激动的挪不开脚步，置身其中，你明显能感受到空气中弥漫着财富，华贵和慵懒的气息。

attractions.text_en: Lake Zurich is 4 kilometers wide and about 39 kilometers long. The docks on the lake are filled with yachts of all kinds, like a rich people's club. One end of Lake Zurich extends deep into the city center. On the lake surface, white swans and wild ducks can be seen everywhere, leisurely and carefree, a harmonious scene. The north bank of Lake Zurich is the residence of the rich, known as the "Gold Coast", while the south bank is called the "Silver Coast". The end of Bahnhofstrasse is Lake Zurich. As one of the "world's most livable cities", the life of true Zurich people is everywhere imbued with tranquility and leisure.
attractions.text_zh: 苏黎世湖宽4公里，长约39公里，湖面码头停满了各色游艇，俨然一个富人俱乐部。苏黎世湖一段的尽头深入市中心，湖面上白天鹅、野鸭随处可见，悠闲自在，一派和谐景象。苏黎世湖的北岸为富人居住地，号称"金岸"，而南岸则称之为"银岸"。班霍夫大街的尽头便是苏黎世湖，作为"世界宜居的城市"，真正的苏黎世人的生活处处渗透着宁静和悠闲。

attractions.text_en: The Grossmünster is one of the 3 main churches of Zurich, Switzerland (the other two are the Fraumünster of Zurich and another). The church is located near the bank of the Limmat River, first built around 1100 and inaugurated around 1220. This church, facing the Fraumünster across the river, played a pivotal role in the Swiss Reformation of the 16th century; both the Faculty of Theology of the University of Zurich and the Museum of the Reformation are established here. The stained-glass paintings inside the church are masterpieces by the Swiss modern art master Giacometti.
attractions.text_zh: 苏黎世大教堂(Grossmünster)是瑞士苏黎世的3座主要教堂之一(另外两座是苏黎世圣母大教堂和)。教堂位于利马特河河岸附近，始建于1100年前后，约1220年揭幕。这座与圣母大教堂隔河相望的教堂在16世纪瑞士宗教改革中，起到了举足轻重的作用，苏黎世大学神学院和宗教改革博物馆都设立于此。教堂内的彩画玻璃是瑞士现代艺术大师贾科梅蒂的杰作。

attractions.text_en: Located between Lucerne and Interlaken, the famous GoldenPass Line train passes through here. Surrounded by mountains, with lush green grass, the quaint village is scattered along the shore of Lake Lungern, like a paradise far from the hustle and bustle of the world.
attractions.text_zh: 地处卢塞恩与因特拉肯之间，著名的金色山口快车就会从这里经过。这里群山环抱，绿草茵茵，古朴的村庄散落于龙疆湖畔，俨然一座远离尘嚣的世外桃源。

attractions.text_en: Lucerne is located in central Switzerland and belongs to the German-speaking region of Switzerland. Nestled between mountains and water, Lucerne possesses the beauty unique to the Middle Ages: medieval churches and towers, Renaissance halls and mansions, as well as century-old shops, long streets and ancient lanes, all abound. The lakes and mountains of Lake Lucerne reflect the beautiful scenery of the city in a way both illusory and real, making it that extraordinary pearl among the many beautiful small cities of Switzerland. Since the 19th century, it has attracted many princes, nobles and literati to come and visit and reside, and artists have found endless artistic inspiration in Lucerne.
attractions.text_zh: 卢塞恩位于瑞士中部，属瑞士德语区。依山傍水的卢塞恩具有中世纪所特有的美：中世纪的教堂、塔楼，文艺复兴时期的宫厅、邸宅以及百年老店、长街古巷，比比皆是。卢塞恩湖的湖光山色更是将城中的美景倒影的亦幻亦真，是瑞士众多美丽小城中不凡的那颗明珠。从19世纪起，就吸引了众多的王公贵族和文人墨客前来游览和居住，艺术家们在卢塞恩得到了不尽的艺术灵感。

attractions.text_en: This lake has a winding shape, and together with the surrounding mountains it forms typical "lake and mountain" scenery, attracting a large number of tourists to come sightseeing. The music critic Ludwig Rellstab once described the misty atmosphere of the first movement of Beethoven's "Piano Sonata No. 14" as the moonlight over Lake Lucerne, and this piece thus acquired the title "Moonlight" - although this was not Beethoven's original intention.
attractions.text_zh: 这座湖泊形状曲折，和周边的群山构成了典型的"湖光山色"景致，吸引了大量游客前来观光。音乐评论家雷尔斯塔布(Ludwig Rellstab)曾将贝多芬《第14钢琴奏鸣曲》的第1乐章的朦胧气氛形容为卢塞恩湖的月色，这首曲子因此得到了《月光》这个标题——虽然这并非贝多芬的本意。

attractions.text_en: This dying lion statue was carved into the natural rock in 1821 by a Danish sculptor. This lion, 10 meters long and more than 3 meters high, lies in agony on the ground, with a broken spear stuck in its shoulder, and beside it is a shield bearing the Swiss coat of arms. This statue was made to commemorate the 786 Swiss mercenaries who all died in battle on August 10, 1792, while protecting the safety of the family of Louis XVI in the Tuileries Palace in Paris. Below the statue, an inscription describes the course of this event. In those days, Switzerland was a poor and backward country, and men, forced by livelihood, went one after another to various European countries to serve as mercenaries. Swiss mercenaries were loyal to their employers, brave and skilled in battle, but honor and money could not conceal the cruelty of the mercenary system. After this event, Switzerland stopped exporting mercenaries, leaving only the guard serving the Holy See, which is that famous Swiss Guard. Owing to their famous loyalty and bravery, this Swiss Guard of the Holy See has served right up to the present. Later, the American writer Mark Twain came to Lucerne and praised the "dying Lion of Lucerne" as "the most mournful and moving statue in the world".
attractions.text_zh: 这只濒死的狮子雕像是1821年由丹麦雕塑家雕刻在天然岩石上的。这头长10米、高3米多的雄狮，痛苦地倒在地上，折断的长矛插在肩头，旁边有一个带有瑞士国徽的盾牌。这座雕像是为了纪念1792年8月10日，为保护巴黎杜乐丽宫(Tuileries)中的路易十六家族的安全，全部战死的786名瑞士雇佣兵，雕像下方有文字描述了此事件的经过。当年，瑞士是一个贫穷落后的国家，男子迫于生计，纷纷到欧洲各国当雇佣兵。瑞士雇佣兵忠于雇主，英勇善战，但荣誉和金钱掩盖不了雇佣兵制度的残酷，这次事件之后，瑞士停止出口雇佣兵，仅留下为天主教廷服务的近卫军，就是那支著名的瑞士卫队。由于著名的忠勇，教廷的这支瑞士近卫军一直服务到现在。后来，美国作家马克·吐温来到卢塞恩，将"濒死的琉森狮子"誉为"世界上最悲壮和最感人的雕像"。

attractions.text_en: Located at the northern foot of the Eiger, Grindelwald is a famous postcard photo-shooting location of the Swiss "dream hillside" that photographers especially love, and is also the Alpine town one must pass through when visiting the Jungfrau. Together with First, Jungfrau, Wengen and others, it forms the more magnificent and beautiful scenic line of the Jungfrau region. In winter it is a famous snow-watching destination, and in summer it is a paradise for hiking enthusiasts. The lakeside, green grasslands, waterfalls, dairy cows and snowy mountains together make up this fascinating town.
attractions.text_zh: 位于艾格峰(Eiger)的北麓脚下的格林德尔瓦尔德(Grindelwald) ，这里有摄影家心头非常爱的瑞士"梦幻山坡"著名明信片拍摄地，也是游览少女峰必经的阿尔卑斯小镇，同菲斯特(First)、少女峰(Jungfrau)、翁根(Wengen)等一起组成了少女峰地区较为壮阔美丽的风景线。冬季是著名的观雪胜地，夏季又是徒步爱好者的天堂，湖畔、绿草青地、瀑布、奶牛、雪山共同构成让这座让人着迷的小镇。

attractions.text_en: Grindelwald First attracts tourists from all directions to come and admire the magnificent scenery of the north slope of the Eiger. Standing on the famous First Cliff Walk by Tissot, anyone will immediately feel that they are standing on top of the world. The Cliff Walk by Tissot is located at the summit of First, where you can take in a unique panorama of the Bernese Alps.
attractions.text_zh: 格林德瓦菲斯特吸引四面八方的游客前来欣赏艾格峰北坡的壮丽风光。站在著名的菲斯特悬崖天梭巅峰栈道，任何人都会立刻感到自己站在世界之巅。悬崖天梭巅峰栈道位于菲斯特峰顶，可以一览伯尔尼兹阿尔卑斯山的独特全景。

attractions.text_en: The Jungfrau is located twenty or thirty kilometers south of Interlaken, at an altitude of 4,158 meters, and is one of the higher peaks of the Alps. The summit is covered with ice and snow all year round, while at the foot of the mountain the grass is green and the scenery is beautiful, making it a famous Swiss sightseeing and outdoor sports destination. The Jungfrau region was listed as a World Natural Heritage Site in 2001.
attractions.text_zh: 少女峰位于因特拉肯以南二三十公里处，海拔 4158 米，是阿尔卑斯山脉的较高的山峰之一。山顶常年被冰雪覆盖，而山下却绿草茵茵、风景秀丽，是瑞士有名的观光及户外运动胜地。少女峰地区已于 2001 年被列为世界自然遗产。

attractions.text_en: Höhematte is the green-space square in the center of the town of Interlaken, a famous Swiss sightseeing town. This green space was the garden of a former monastery, and is now prohibited from any construction of buildings, in order to prevent damage to this beautiful landscape. This is a good place to gaze at the Jungfrau. On the vast green space there is a fountain, also dotted with many benches, so it is also a good place for strolling. Höhematte is also a landing site for hang-gliding; there is a reception point at the edge of the lawn, and after paying, staff will take tourists to a nearby mountain for free gliding, from which you can admire the beautiful scenery of Interlaken from high in the air.
attractions.text_zh: 荷黑马特是瑞士著名的观光小镇因特拉肯镇中心的绿地广场，这片绿地是昔日修道院的庭园，现在则禁止兴建任何建筑物，以防止破坏这片美丽的景观。这里是眺望少女峰好的场所，辽阔的绿地上建有喷泉，还点缀着很多凉椅，所以也是散步的好地方。荷黑马特还是滑翔翼的降落地，草坪边上有接待点，付费后会有工作人员把游客带到附近山上进行自由滑翔，可在高空欣赏到因特拉肯的美景。

attractions.text_en: Lake Geneva, also known as Lake Léman, straddles Switzerland and France. It is an important lake of Western Europe, and also a famous scenic area and health resort. During the day you can take a pleasure boat to tour Lake Geneva, and in the evening be intoxicated by the flickering neon lights along the shore. There are also often luxury cruise ships holding concerts or balls on the lake surface.
