attractions.text_en: Notre-Dame de Paris Cathedral sits on the Île de la Cité in the center of Paris and is the cathedral of the Catholic Archdiocese of Paris. The church is in the Gothic style; admire the 17th-century great bell tower, the chimeras on the church's exterior walls, and the beautiful scenery of the Île de la Cité and the Seine. (If a religious activity or Mass is taking place, please do not enter and disturb the worshippers. Notre-Dame de Paris has no admission ticket; if you cannot go inside to visit, you may view the exterior.)
attractions.text_zh: 巴黎圣母院大教堂坐落于巴黎市中心的西岱岛上，是天主教巴黎总教区的主教座堂。教堂为哥特式风格和17世纪大钟楼、教堂外壁上的神兽、领略西岱岛与塞纳河的美丽风光。（如遇宗教活动或者弥撒，请勿入场打扰信徒，巴黎圣母院无门票，如无法入内参观则游览外观。）

attractions.text_en: If the Louvre bore witness to the glory of the Gauls, then the Île de la Cité, Notre-Dame de Paris, and the Hôtel de Ville (Paris City Hall) wrote down their inseparable bond with the Romans who conquered them. Without the Romans, there would be no Île de la Cité. Without the Île de la Cité, there would be no Paris as it is today.
attractions.text_zh: 如果说卢浮宫见证了高卢人的荣耀，那西岱岛，巴黎圣母院和巴黎市政厅则写下了与征服她们的罗马人的不解之缘。如果没有罗马人，就没有西岱岛。没有西岱岛，就没有现在的巴黎。

attractions.text_en: The Palace of Versailles has always been a magnificent monument of the era of feudal rule in France. In substance it was not only the French court but also the administrative center of the nation, and a concrete embodiment of the social and political views and lifestyle of France at the time. It represents the power of an absolutist regime able to concentrate such enormous manpower, material resources, and finances on a scale unseen in Europe since the ancient Roman Empire. At that time, in order to build it, Louis XIV mobilized more than 30,000 workers along with architects, engineers, and technicians. In addition to solving the complex technical problems arising from constructing such a large-scale building complex, they also had to solve problems in water supply, roads, and other areas. It can thus be seen that the success of the Palace of Versailles powerfully proves the economic and technological progress of France at the time as well as the wisdom of its working people.
attractions.text_zh: 凡尔赛宫始终是法国封建统治历史时期的一座华丽的纪念碑。从内容上讲不仅是法兰西宫廷，而且是国家的行政中心，也是当时法国社会政治观点、生活方式的具体体现。它是欧洲自古罗马帝国以来表现出能够集中如此巨大的人力、物力、财力的专制政体力量。当时，路易十四为了建造它，共动用了三万余名工人和建筑师、工程师、技师，除了要解决建造大规模建筑群所产生的复杂技术问题外，还要解决引水、道路等各方面的问题。可见，凡尔赛宫的成功，有力地证明了当时法国经济和技术的进步和劳动人民的智慧。

attractions.text_en: Boulevard Haussmann lies between the 8th and 9th arrondissements of Paris. On the street are the two great department stores Galeries Lafayette and Printemps. Marcel Proust, the author of "In Search of Lost Time," completed most of the writing of "In Search of Lost Time" in his residence at No. 102 on this street.
attractions.text_zh: 奥斯曼大街位于巴黎第八区和第九区之间。街上有巴黎老佛爷百货公司和春天百货两大百货公司。《追忆似水年华》的作者马塞尔·普鲁斯特曾在这条街102号的居所里，完成了《追忆似水年华》的大部分写作。

attractions.text_en: The Paris Opera was designed by Charles Garnier in 1861. Its architecture perfectly combines several architectural forms, including the ancient Greco-Roman colonnade and the Baroque. Grand in scale, exquisitely detailed, and resplendent, it is hailed as a theater in which painting, marble, and gold ornamentation set each other off, giving people great enjoyment. It is one of the typical buildings of Napoleon III's era. (If the Paris Opera has an important performance or reception that makes it impossible to go inside to visit, and our agency cannot accurately predict the public opening hours, therefore the 15-euro admission ticket is not included; if it is open for visiting, you may purchase a ticket on site yourself.)
attractions.text_zh: 巴黎歌剧院是由查尔斯·加尼叶于1861年设计的，其建筑将古希腊罗马式柱廊、巴洛克等几种建筑形式完美地结合在一起，规模宏大，精美细致，金碧辉煌，被誉为是一座绘画、大理石和金饰交相辉映的剧院，给人以极大的享受，是拿破仑三世典型的建筑之一。（巴黎歌剧院如有重要演出活动或接待活动无法入内参观，我社无法准确预测公众开放时间，因此、不包含门票15欧元，如参观接待，可在现场自行购买门票。）

attractions.text_en: Located in the Burgundy region, the home of wine, Dijon was the capital of the Duchy of Burgundy from the 11th to the 15th century. It flourished especially in the 14th and 15th centuries thanks to several outstanding leaders, becoming an important political, economic, and cultural and artistic center of Europe at the time. Today, the old town of Dijon is still dotted with many historic buildings from different periods. In addition to luxurious palaces, solemn churches, sumptuous glazed-tile roofs, and many quaint timber-framed houses, there are also museums and art galleries converted from palaces and monasteries. Fine wine and fine food paired with an artistic atmosphere throughout the city make it a city full of French character.
attractions.text_zh: 位于葡萄酒故乡勃艮第地区的第戎，11到-15世纪曾是勃艮第公国的首府，特别在14、15世纪时因几位优秀领导人而盛极一时，成为当时欧洲重要的政治经济和文化艺术中心。如今第戎的老城区仍散布着许多不同时期的历史建筑，除了豪华的宫殿、庄严的教堂、彩釉屋顶的豪华和许多古朴的木框架房屋，也有宫殿和修道院改装成的博物馆和美术馆。美酒、美食搭配满城艺术分为，是非常具有法国风情的一座城市。

attractions.text_en: Notre-Dame de Dijon was built in the 13th century. On the church's facade, three rows of gargoyle-shaped false waterspouts are separated by two rows of slender columns, and the church has a 14th-century clock that strikes the hours. For most of the people of Dijon, Notre-Dame de Dijon is a church building with great approachability; over these recent centuries, the church has always been a main source of spiritual and emotional comfort for the people of Dijon.
attractions.text_zh: 第戎圣母院建造于13世纪，教堂门面上三列兽形假排水口由两排纤细的圆柱分开，教堂上有一座14世纪的报时钟。对于大多数的第戎人民而言，第戎圣母院是一座具有亲和力的教堂建筑，在这近几个世纪以来，圣母院一直是第戎人民主要的精神、心灵的抚慰。

attractions.text_en: This destination, which combines winemaking and tourism in one, is a place not to be missed by wine lovers, art lovers, and food lovers. Choose a winery producing Burgundy products for a visit, where you can see the aging and maturation process of wine and the storage and fermentation process inside the cellar; if you are lucky, you may see the winemaking process of the wine (depending on the grape season).
attractions.text_zh: 这个集酿酒与旅游于一身的胜地是葡萄酒爱好者、艺术爱好者和美食爱好者所不容错过的地方。选择勃艮第产品的酒庄进行参观，在此可参观葡萄酒的陈酿熟化过程及酒窖内储存发酵过程，运气好的时候可参观葡萄酒的酿造过程（取决葡萄的产期）。

attractions.text_en: Lausanne is the capital of the Canton of Vaud, located at the center of the northern shore of Lake Geneva. It prospered because it was once a Roman military port. Through continuous development, this modern city of Lausanne has become one of the most important railway hubs in Switzerland. Most trains heading to major cities such as Geneva, Bern, and Zurich converge here, and it is also a transport center connecting Paris and Milan. Lausanne is also known as the Olympic Capital; the International Olympic Committee and the Olympic Museum are both located here.
attractions.text_zh: 洛桑是沃州的首府，位于日内瓦湖畔北部沿岸的中心，因曾是罗马的军港而繁荣。经过不断地发展，洛桑这座现代化的城市已经成为瑞士最重要的铁路枢纽之一。大多数通往日内瓦、伯尔尼、苏黎世等大城市的火车都在此交汇，它同时也是连接巴黎及米兰的交通中心。洛桑也被人们称为奥林匹克之都，国际奥林匹克委员会和奥林匹克博物馆均在此地。

attractions.text_en: Lausanne is the city designated by Baron de Coubertin, the father of the modern Olympics, as the base of the International Olympic Committee (IOC). To carry on Coubertin's will of spreading the Olympic spirit throughout the world, the world's first Olympic Museum was established in Lausanne in 1993, designated by former IOC President Samaranch. The Lausanne Olympic Museum collects and displays tens of thousands of treasured items from the first Olympic Games in Athens to the present, and is a resource center for the Olympic movement. The museum sits on the picturesque shore of Lake Geneva in an excellent setting; from the lush courtyard or the restaurant terrace you can gaze upon the beautiful lake and mountain scenery of Lausanne.
attractions.text_zh: 洛桑是近代奥林匹克之父顾拜旦（Coubertin）男爵为国际奥林匹克委员会（IOC）根据地所指定的城市，为继承顾拜旦将奥林匹克的精神传播到世界各地的意志，由前任奥委会主席萨马兰奇指定，于1993年在洛桑建立了世界上一座奥林匹克博物馆。洛桑奥林匹克博物馆（Olympic Museum）收藏并展示了从第一届雅典奥运会至今的上万件珍藏品，是奥林匹克运动资料中心。博物馆坐落在风光绰约的日内瓦湖畔，环境极佳，从葱郁的庭院或餐厅的阳台上可以眺望洛桑美丽的湖光山色。

attractions.text_en: The Lavaux Vineyard Terraces, inscribed on the World Cultural Heritage List, have a history of 800 years to this day. With ample sunshine and the humid lakeside climate, the grapes produced here are not only very sweet, but the area has also become a famous wine-producing region of Switzerland. In the season when the grapes ripen, the air is filled with the fragrance of wine.
attractions.text_zh: 被列入世界文化遗产名录的拉沃葡萄园梯田，迄今为止已有800年的历史。凭借充足的阳光和湖畔湿润的气候，不但让这里产出的葡萄非常甘甜，而且还使当地成为瑞士著名的葡萄酒产地。葡萄成熟的季节，空气里都弥漫着葡萄酒的香气。

attractions.text_en: Lake Geneva, also known as Lake Léman, straddles Switzerland and France. It is an important lake of Western Europe and also a famous scenic area and health resort. During the day you can take a cruise boat to tour Lake Geneva, and at night be intoxicated by the twinkling neon lights along the shore; there are also often luxury cruise ships holding concerts or dances on the lake. You can take a cruise boat to appreciate the beautiful scenery along the shores of Lake Geneva, watching the lake water, snowy mountains, and blue sky merge into one.
attractions.text_zh: 日内瓦湖又名莱芒湖，横跨瑞士和法国，是西欧的重要湖泊，也是著名的风景区和疗养地。白天你可以乘坐游船游览日内瓦湖，晚上陶醉于岸边闪烁的霓虹，还时常有豪华游轮在湖面上举办音乐会或舞会。可乘坐游船领略日内瓦湖岸边美景，看湖水、雪山和蓝天连成一片。

attractions.text_en: Chillon Castle is located at the foot of the Alps on the charming shore of Lake Geneva, and is Switzerland's iconic medieval "floating-on-water" castle. The foundation stones of Chillon Castle are built on the lake bed, giving people the feeling that it floats on the surface of the lake, yet the castle looks exceptionally solid. Byron's poem "The Prisoner of Chillon" made this castle even more famous.
attractions.text_zh: 西庸城堡位于阿尔卑斯山脚下，充满魅力的日内瓦湖湖畔，是瑞士标志性的中世纪“水上漂”城堡。西庸城堡的基石建在湖底，给人一种漂浮在湖面上的感觉，但城堡看起来异常坚固，拜伦挥笔下的《西庸的囚徒》更是让这座城堡声名远扬。

attractions.text_en: Lake Thun, set on the Bernese Highlands, has deep blue waters and is called "the right eye of God." Standing on the shore of Lake Thun, before your eyes are beautiful villages, a solemn castle, and at the far end of the lake the gradually emerging peaks of the Eiger, the Mönch, and the Jungfrau. Your gaze switches between telephoto and wide-angle, capturing in your heart each frame of picture-like scenery.
attractions.text_zh: 坐落于伯尔尼高地上的图恩湖，拥有深蓝色的湖水，被称之为“上帝的右眼”。驻足于图恩湖畔，眼前的美丽村落，庄严的城堡，湖畔尽头的慢慢浮现的艾格峰、僧侣峰和少女峰，视线在长焦与广角之间切换，在心中定格每一帧如画风景。

attractions.text_en: The Jungfrau is located about 20 to 30 kilometers south of Interlaken, at an altitude of 4,158 meters, and is one of the higher peaks of the Alps. The summit is covered with ice and snow year-round, while at the foot of the mountain the grass is green and the scenery is beautiful, making it a famous sightseeing and outdoor sports destination in Switzerland. The Jungfrau region was inscribed as a World Natural Heritage in 2001.
attractions.text_zh: 少女峰位于因特拉肯以南二三十公里处，海拔 4158 米，是阿尔卑斯山脉的较高的山峰之一。山顶常年被冰雪覆盖，而山下却绿草茵茵、风景秀丽，是瑞士有名的观光及户外运动胜地。少女峰地区已于 2001 年被列为世界自然遗产。

attractions.text_en: Located at the northern foot of the Eiger, Grindelwald is home to the famous postcard photography spot of Switzerland's "dream slope" beloved by photographers, and it is also the Alpine town one must pass through when visiting the Jungfrau. Together with First, the Jungfrau, Wengen, Lauterbrunnen, and others, it forms the most magnificent and beautiful scenic route of the Jungfrau region. In winter it is a famous snow-viewing destination, and in summer it is a paradise for hiking enthusiasts; lakesides, green grassy lands, waterfalls, dairy cows, and snowy mountains together make up this captivating town.
attractions.text_zh: 位于艾格峰(Eiger)的北麓脚下的格林德尔瓦尔德(Grindelwald) ，这里有摄影家心头最爱的瑞士“梦幻山坡”著名明信片拍摄地，也是游览少女峰必经的阿尔卑斯小镇，同菲斯特(First)、少女峰(Jungfrau)、翁根(Wengen)、劳特布龙嫩（Lauterbrunnen）等一起组成了少女峰地区最为壮阔美丽的风景线。冬季是著名的观雪胜地，夏季又是徒步爱好者的天堂，湖畔、绿草青地、瀑布、奶牛、雪山共同构成让这座让人着迷的小镇。

attractions.text_en: Lauterbrunnen is located to the west of the Jungfrau region. More than 70 waterfalls and small rivers tumble down from the cliffs, so the literal German meaning of Lauterbrunnen is "many springs," and this town is a true waterfall town. Among them, the Staubbach Falls is the village's iconic sight, praised in poetry by the poets Goethe and Wordsworth. In addition, there are the Mürren Falls and the Trümmelbach Falls, which rush down from a cave ten stories high, also not to be missed. From Lauterbrunnen you can take the WAB railway to Wengen, Kleine Scheidegg, the Jungfrau, and other places. At the same time, by taking the cable car via Mürren, you can reach the Schilthorn and other places, taking in the majestic scenery of the Bernese Highlands.
attractions.text_zh: 劳特布龙嫩(Lauterbrunnen)位于少女峰地区的西面，从山崖上落下来70多个瀑布和小河，所以劳特布龙嫩的德语直译是很多的泉水，这个小镇是名副其实的瀑布镇。其中施陶河瀑布是村子的标志性景观，诗人歌德和伍德·沃斯曾写诗来赞美它。此外还有米伦瀑布和从十层楼高的山洞里飞奔而下的特吕默尔河瀑布，也不可错过。从劳特布龙嫩出发可以乘WAB铁路到达翁根、克莱纳谢德格、少女峰等地。同时，乘坐缆车经米伦，可到达雪朗峰等地，饱览伯尔尼高地的雄伟风光。

attractions.text_en: Lake Brienz, at the northern foot of the Alps, has waters that are blue tinged with green, called "Tiffany high-grade blue" waters, and enjoys the reputation of "the left eye of God." Lake Brienz has the purest lake water in Switzerland. The book "Around the World (Switzerland)" describes it thus: "An extremely charming and tranquil village, with delightful streets and architecture, azure lake water, mysterious lakes, dense forests, lovely steam locomotives, and a panorama of the Alps — all the various charms of Switzerland are concentrated here"......
attractions.text_zh: 阿尔卑斯山北麓的布里恩茨湖，湖水蓝中带绿，被称为Tiffany高级蓝的湖水，有着“上帝的左眼”的美誉。布里恩茨湖是瑞士最为纯净的湖水，《走遍全球（瑞士）》书上这么描述：“一个极富风情的恬静村子，富有情趣的街道建筑、蔚蓝色的湖水、神秘的湖泊、茂密的森林、可爱的蒸汽机车和阿尔卑斯全景图，瑞士的各种魅力在这里浓缩”......

attractions.text_en: Höhematte is the green meadow square in the center of Interlaken, Switzerland's famous tourist town. This green space was once the garden of a monastery, and now the construction of any buildings on it is forbidden, in order to prevent damage to this beautiful landscape. This is the best place to gaze at the Jungfrau; on the vast green there are fountains and many benches dotted about, so it is also a good place for a stroll. Höhematte is also a landing site for hang-gliders; there is a reception point at the edge of the lawn, and after paying, staff will take visitors to nearby mountains for free gliding, allowing you to admire the beautiful scenery of Interlaken from high in the sky.
attractions.text_zh: 荷黑马特是瑞士著名的观光小镇因特拉肯镇中心的绿地广场，这片绿地是昔日修道院的庭园，现在则禁止兴建任何建筑物，以防止破坏这片美丽的景观。这里是眺望少女峰好的场所，辽阔的绿地上建有喷泉，还点缀着很多凉椅，所以也是散步的好地方。荷黑马特还是滑翔翼的降落地，草坪边上有接待点，付费后会有工作人员把游客带到附近山上进行自由滑翔，可在高空欣赏到因特拉肯的美景。

attractions.text_en: Interlaken is a town at the foot of the Jungfrau, located between Lake Thun and Lake Brienz, also known as the "town between the lakes." It is famous for its enchanting scenery throughout the four seasons, and geographically it belongs to the center of the Bernese Highlands.
attractions.text_zh: 因特拉肯是少女峰山脚下的一个小镇，位于图恩湖及布里恩茨湖之间，又名湖间镇，以一年四季的醉人风景著名，在地理上它属于伯尔尼高地的中心。

attractions.text_en: Switzerland's GoldenPass train shuttles through the beautiful regions of the Alps, taking in charming lake and mountain scenery all along the way. One of the most beautiful sections is the GoldenPass Line stretch from Lungern to Lucerne (Luzern-Lungern). Along the way, the lovely villages, the azure lake water, the lush vegetation, the vast pastures, the leisurely cattle and sheep, and the snow-capped Alps, with the scenery changing from the foot of the mountains to the summit, make people sigh that this is truly a fairyland on earth.
attractions.text_zh: 瑞士的黄金列车穿梭于阿尔卑斯美丽的区域，一路总览迷人的湖光山色。其中美丽的一段就是龙疆（Lungern）到卢塞恩（Luzern）这一段的金色山口列车（Luzren-Lungern），沿途秀美的村庄，碧蓝的湖水，茂密的植被，广袤的牧场，悠闲的牛羊，还有白雪皑皑的阿尔卑斯山脉，从山脚到山顶变幻的风景，让人感叹这就是人间仙境。

attractions.text_en: Lucerne is located in central Switzerland and belongs to the German-speaking region of Switzerland. Nestled against the mountains and beside the water, Lucerne possesses the unique beauty of the Middle Ages: medieval churches and towers, Renaissance halls and mansions, as well as century-old shops and long streets and ancient alleys, can be found everywhere. The lake and mountain scenery of Lake Lucerne reflects the city's beautiful views as if real and unreal, making it an extraordinary pearl among Switzerland's many beautiful small towns. Since the 19th century, it has attracted numerous princes, nobles, and men of letters to visit and reside, and artists have found inexhaustible artistic inspiration in Lucerne.
attractions.text_zh: 卢塞恩位于瑞士中部，属瑞士德语区。依山傍水的卢塞恩具有中世纪所特有的美：中世纪的教堂、塔楼，文艺复兴时期的宫厅、邸宅以及百年老店、长街古巷，比比皆是。卢塞恩湖的湖光山色更是将城中的美景倒影的亦幻亦真，是瑞士众多美丽小城中不凡的那颗明珠。从19世纪起，就吸引了众多的王公贵族和文人墨客前来游览和居住，艺术家们在卢塞恩得到了不尽的艺术灵感。

attractions.text_en: Situated between Lucerne and Interlaken, the famous GoldenPass Line train passes through here. This place has the purest alpine lake water in Switzerland, surrounded by mountains with lush green grass; the quaint village is scattered along the shore of Lake Lungern, just like a Shangri-La far from the hustle and bustle of the world.
attractions.text_zh: 地处卢塞恩与因特拉肯之间，著名的金色山口列车就会从这里经过。这里拥有瑞士最为纯净的高山湖水，群山环抱，绿草茵茵，古朴的村庄散落于龙疆湖畔，俨然一座远离尘嚣的世外桃源。

attractions.text_en: Lake Lucerne has a winding shape, and together with the surrounding mountains it forms the typical scenery of "lake and mountain views," attracting large numbers of tourists to come sightseeing. The music critic Ludwig Rellstab once likened the hazy atmosphere of the first movement of Beethoven's "Piano Sonata No. 14" to the moonlight on Lake Lucerne, and so the piece acquired the title "Moonlight" — although this was not Beethoven's original intention.
attractions.text_zh: 卢塞恩湖这座湖泊形状曲折，和周边的群山构成了典型的“湖光山色”景致，吸引了大量游客前来观光。音乐评论家雷尔斯塔布(Ludwig Rellstab)曾将贝多芬《第14钢琴奏鸣曲》的第1乐章的朦胧气氛形容为卢塞恩湖的月色，这首曲子因此得到了《月光》这个标题——虽然这并非贝多芬的本意。

attractions.text_en: This dying lion statue was carved into natural rock in 1821 by a Danish sculptor. This lion, 10 meters long and over 3 meters high, lies in agony on the ground, a broken spear thrust into its shoulder, with a shield bearing the Swiss coat of arms beside it. This statue was made to commemorate the 786 Swiss mercenaries who all died in battle on 10 August 1792 to protect the safety of the family of Louis XVI in the Tuileries Palace in Paris; below the statue is text describing the course of this event. In those years, Switzerland was a poor and backward country, and men, driven by the need to make a living, went to various European countries to serve as mercenaries. Swiss mercenaries were loyal to their employers and brave and skilled in battle, but the honor and money could not mask the cruelty of the mercenary system. After this event, Switzerland stopped exporting mercenaries, leaving only the guard serving the Holy See of the Catholic Church — that famous Swiss Guard. Owing to their renowned loyalty and bravery, this Swiss Guard of the Holy See has served right up to the present day. Later, the American writer Mark Twain came to Lucerne and hailed the "dying lion of Lucerne" as "the most mournful and moving piece of stone in the world."
attractions.text_zh: 这只濒死的狮子雕像是1821年由丹麦雕塑家雕刻在天然岩石上的。这头长10米、高3米多的雄狮，痛苦地倒在地上，折断的长矛插在肩头，旁边有一个带有瑞士国徽的盾牌。这座雕像是为了纪念1792年8月10日，为保护巴黎杜乐丽宫(Tuileries)中的路易十六家族的安全，全部战死的786名瑞士雇佣兵，雕像下方有文字描述了此事件的经过。当年，瑞士是一个贫穷落后的国家，男子迫于生计，纷纷到欧洲各国当雇佣兵。瑞士雇佣兵忠于雇主，英勇善战，但荣誉和金钱掩盖不了雇佣兵制度的残酷，这次事件之后，瑞士停止出口雇佣兵，仅留下为天主教廷服务的近卫军，就是那支著名的瑞士卫队。由于著名的忠勇，教廷的这支瑞士近卫军一直服务到现在。后来，美国作家马克·吐温来到卢塞恩，将“濒死的琉森狮子”誉为“世界上最悲壮和最感人的雕像”。

attractions.text_en: The old town of Zurich is an experience not to be missed; here gather traditional craftsmen and antique dealers, bookstores and flower shops, as well as jewelry and accessories, fashion boutiques, and of course Zurich's own local brands. Famous streets and lanes such as Bahnhofstrasse, Rennweg, Niederdorf, and Augustine Lane are all located in the old town. Strolling through the old town at night, you will find that this place once again becomes a place of entertainment for the public.
attractions.text_zh: 苏黎世的老城区是个不可错过的体验之地，这里汇聚了传统工匠与古董商、书店和花铺，也有珠宝首饰，时尚精品，当然也少不了苏黎世本土品牌。班霍夫大街(Bahnhofstrasse)、伦韦格街(Rennweg)、尼德道尔夫(Niederdorf)与圣奥古斯丁巷(Augustine Lane)等著名街巷都位于老城区。晚上漫步于老城区，会发现这里又变成了民众的娱乐场所。

attractions.text_en: Lake Zurich is 4 kilometers wide and about 39 kilometers long. The docks on the lake surface are filled with yachts of every kind, just like a club for the rich. One end of a section of Lake Zurich extends deep into the city center, and on the lake white swans and wild ducks can be seen everywhere, leisurely and carefree, presenting a harmonious scene. The north shore of Lake Zurich is the residence of the wealthy, known as the "Gold Coast," while the south shore is called the "Silver Coast." At the end of Bahnhofstrasse is Lake Zurich; as one of the "world's most livable cities," the lives of true Zurich residents are imbued everywhere with tranquility and leisure.
attractions.text_zh: 苏黎世湖宽4公里，长约39公里，湖面码头停满了各色游艇，俨然一个富人俱乐部。苏黎世湖一段的尽头深入市中心，湖面上白天鹅、野鸭随处可见，悠闲自在，一派和谐景象。苏黎世湖的北岸为富人居住地，号称“金岸”，而南岸则称之为“银岸”。班霍夫大街的尽头便是苏黎世湖，作为“世界宜居的城市”，真正的苏黎世人的生活处处渗透着宁静和悠闲。

attractions.text_en: Lindenhof, meaning "the courtyard of the linden trees," is a park sitting atop a small hill covered with linden trees in the center of Zurich's old town. Right before you is the Limmat River, making it an ideal place to admire the Grossmünster (Zurich's great cathedral) and the scenery of the right bank. It is also where, at the start of the main story of "Crash Landing on You," the perfect frame was captured of the two protagonists passing each other by.
attractions.text_zh: 林登霍夫(Lindenhof)，意思为菩提树的中庭，公园坐落于苏黎世老城中心一片被菩提树覆盖的小山丘上。眼前就是利马特河，是欣赏苏黎世大教堂和右岸风光的理想之地。也是为《爱的迫降》的正片开始时，定格了两人擦身而过的完美的画面。

attractions.text_en: The Limmat River is drawn out from the northern end of Lake Zurich, then flows through the center of Zurich, and after that flows 35 kilometers to the northwest, finally emptying into the Aare River. Walking along the bank of the Limmat, watching the slowly flowing river water, it is another quiet and peaceful day — this is probably the feeling of "the good times of quiet years." In the river, swans inadvertently intrude when tourists are taking photos, adding a touch of liveliness and playfulness to this calm river.
attractions.text_zh: 利马特河从苏黎世湖北端引出，然后流经苏黎世市中心，再向西北方流35公里，最后注入阿勒河（Aare）。行走在利马特河边，看着缓慢流动的河水，又是静谧而祥和的一天，这大概就是岁月静好的感觉，河里天鹅在游人拍照时不经意的闯入，又为这平静的河水增添一丝灵动和跳脱之感。

attractions.text_en: Although its name Duomo means "dome," Milan Cathedral is in fact a Gothic building. Dominating the city center, Milan Cathedral was begun in 1386 and took 500 years to complete. Hailed as Italy's magnificent Gothic architecture, its hundreds of spires soar into the clouds, and its white marble exterior walls are carved with thousands of statues. The interior space is vast; sunlight streams in through the beautiful stained-glass mosaic pictures on the windows, the light is hazy, and the atmosphere is full of solemn religiousness. In the church's crypt, remains of the Church of Santa Tecla, which stood on the original site, are still preserved.
attractions.text_zh: 虽然它的名字Duomo是“圆顶”的意思，米兰大教堂实际上却是一座哥特式建筑。雄踞在市中心的米兰大教堂始建于1386年，历经500年才完工，被誉为意大利壮丽的哥特式建筑数以百计的尖塔耸入云霄，白色大理石外墙上雕刻着数千座雕像。内部空间宽广，阳光透过窗上美丽的彩色玻璃镶嵌画射入，光线朦胧，充满庄严的宗教气氛。教堂地下室中还保留着原址上的圣·特库拉教堂的遗迹。

attractions.text_en: This cross-shaped glass-vaulted gallery built in the 19th century is not only one of the oldest shopping centers in the world, but also a city-scale model of art and architecture. Here gather the flagship stores of Italian luxury brands such as Prada and Gucci, but even if you do not shop, strolling through it is like walking through a museum. At the old-established cafés at the end of the gallery, such as Caffè Biffi or Savini Milano, you can order an espresso or taste a tiramisu dessert, experiencing the composure and elegance of the Italian pace of life.
attractions.text_zh: 这条19世纪修建的十字形玻璃拱顶长廊不仅是世界上最古老的购物中心之一，更是一座城市级的艺术与建筑典范。这里汇聚了Prada、Gucci等意大利奢侈品牌旗舰店，但即便不购物，漫步其中也如同穿行于一座博物馆。在长廊尽头的老字号咖啡馆，如Caffè Biffi或Savini Milano，您可点一杯意式浓缩咖啡或品尝提拉米苏甜点，体会意式生活节奏的从容与优雅。

attractions.name_zh: 威尼斯圣马可大教堂（外观）
attractions.text_en:
This magnificent church in Byzantine style is famous for its five onion-shaped domes and golden mosaic decorations, embodying the glorious history of Venice's golden age and its trade with the East.
The exterior is rich in detail, blending the artistic languages of East and West, and it is a landmark of Venice not to be missed.
attractions.text_zh:
这座拜占庭风格的华丽教堂，以其五座洋葱形圆顶和金色马赛克装饰而闻名，体现了威尼斯黄金时代与东方贸易的辉煌历史。
外观细节丰富，融合东方与西方的艺术语言，是威尼斯不可错过的地标。

attractions.text_en: This is a museum located in Piazza San Marco, where all of Venice's past glory resides. Inside, what you can see is not only frescoes and the like, but also medieval weapons; on the way to the dungeon you walk across the Bridge of Sighs, where you can look outside through small holes, rather than having as many people on the opposite bridge looking over at you. Inside you are allowed to take photos, as long as you do not use a flash.
attractions.text_zh: 这是一家位于Piazza San Marco的博物馆，威尼斯过去的辉煌都在这里了。里面可以看的不仅有壁画等，还有中世纪的兵器，去地牢的时候走过叹息桥，能通过小孔看外面，而不是像对面桥上面那么多的人在往你这里看叹息桥。里面是可以拍照片的，只要你不要用闪光灯就可以了。

attractions.text_en: The Bridge of Sighs was in ancient times the route that death-row prisoners had to pass when being escorted from the court to the prison. The Bridge of Sighs is in the early Baroque style; the bridge is shaped like a house, with its upper part covered by a vault and very tightly enclosed, having only two small windows on the side facing the canal. After a convict was tried at the Doge's Palace, the serious criminal would be taken to the dungeon, and when crossing this airtight bridge, he could only look at the blue sky through the small windows. From then on he would lose his freedom and could not help letting out a sigh. It is said that lovers who kiss beneath the bridge can be together forever, and the film "A Little Romance" was shot here.
attractions.text_zh: 叹息桥是古代由法院向监狱押送死囚的必经之路。叹息桥造型属早期巴洛克式风格，桥呈房屋状，上部穹隆覆盖，封闭得很严实，只有向运河一侧有两个小窗，当犯人在总督府接受审判之后，重罪犯被带到地牢中，在经过这座密不透气的的桥时，只能透过小窗看看蓝天，从此失去了自由，不自主的发出叹息之声。听说恋人们在桥下接吻就可以天长地久，而电影「情定日落桥」就在这取景的。

attractions.text_en: Piazza San Marco (St. Mark's Square), also called the central square of Venice, has always been the center of Venice's political, religious, and traditional festival public activities. St. Mark's Square is a rectangular square enclosed by the Doge's Palace, St. Mark's Basilica, the St. Mark's Campanile (bell tower), the New and Old Procuratie buildings, the Napoleonic Wing connecting the two buildings, the four-sided bell tower of St. Mark's Basilica, the Marciana Library, and other buildings, together with the Grand Canal of Venice. It is about 170 meters long, about 80 meters wide on the east side, and about 55 meters wide on the west side. The buildings surrounding the square are all exquisite Renaissance architecture.
attractions.text_zh: 圣马可广场(Plazza San Marco) 又称威尼斯中心广场，一直是威尼斯的政治、宗教和传统节日的公共活动中心。圣马可广场是由公爵府、圣马可大教堂、圣马可钟楼、新、旧行政官邸大楼、连接两大楼的拿破仑翼大楼、圣马可大教堂的四角形钟楼和圣马可图书馆等建筑和威尼斯大运河所围成的长方形广场，长约170米，东边宽约80米，西侧宽约55米。广场四周的建筑都是文艺复兴时期的精美建筑。

attractions.name_zh: 圣母百花大教堂（需排队进入）
attractions.text_en: There are many solemn and magnificent churches in the world, but very few can be so enchanting. This beautiful church, faced with white, red, and green granite, interprets to the fullest the classicism, elegance, and freedom esteemed in the Renaissance era — no wonder it was named "Santa Maria del Fiore" (Saint Mary of the Flower). The original site of Florence Cathedral was the Church of Santa Reparata, built in the 4th century. In 1296, Giovanni de' Medici funded the construction of the new church, which took 175 years to complete.
attractions.text_zh: 世界上庄严雄伟的教堂很多，但很少有教堂能如此妩媚。这座使用白、红、绿三色花岗岩贴面的美丽教堂将文艺复兴时代所推崇的古典、优雅、自由诠释得淋漓尽致，难怪会被命名为“花之圣母”。圣母百花大教堂原址是建于4世纪的圣·雷帕拉塔教堂。1296年，乔凡尼·美第奇出资建造新的教堂，花了175年时间才建成。

attractions.text_en: Piazza della Signoria enjoys the reputation of being an open-air sculpture museum, and for several centuries it has been the center of the city's political life. On the square stand the Palazzo Vecchio (Old Palace), a replica of Michelangelo's sculpture "David," and the Fountain of Neptune. The open Piazza della Signoria is surrounded by the Uffizi Gallery, the Loggia dei Lanzi, and numerous cafés and bars. The famous Florence Cathedral is located directly to its north.
attractions.text_zh: 市政广场有露天雕塑博物馆的美誉，也是该城数个世纪以来政治生活中心，广场上坐落着维琪奥宫（旧宫）、米开朗基罗雕塑作品《大卫》的复制品以及海神喷泉。开放式的市政广场，周围环绕着乌菲兹美术馆、佣兵凉亭和众多的咖啡厅、酒吧。著名的圣母百花大教堂就位于它的正北方。

attractions.text_en: Located on the Arno River, the Ponte Vecchio is also known as the Old Bridge. Built in 1345, it is one of the earlier large-span circular-arch bridges in Europe. It is said that Dante encountered the maiden Beatrice here, and later poured his feelings of longing into "The Divine Comedy." Over several hundred years, it has stood unfallen through floods, social upheavals, and wars it has experienced. It is one of the famous landmark buildings of Florence.
attractions.text_zh: 位于阿诺河(Arno)上的维奇奥桥，又被称为老桥。建于1345年，是欧洲较早的大跨度圆弧拱桥，据说但丁在这里邂逅了少女比阿特丽丝，后来将思慕之情倾注到《神曲》之中。几百年间，它经历过的水灾、社会动乱、战争而屹立不倒。是佛罗伦萨的著名地标建筑之一。

attractions.text_en: Located to the southeast of Florence, Piazzale Michelangelo is a famous spot for overlooking the beautiful panorama of the city. In the center of the square is placed a replica of Michelangelo's immortal masterpiece — "David." From the square you can see the appearance of the whole city: Florence Cathedral, Giotto's Campanile, the Bargello Museum, the Old Bridge, and more all come into view. This is also a good position for viewing sunrise and sunset; as the light changes, watch Florence glow with different splendor.
attractions.text_zh: 位于佛罗伦萨东南的米开朗基罗广场是著名的俯瞰城市美丽全景的地点，广场中央安放着米开朗基罗的不朽之作——《大卫》的复制品，广场上可以看到全城的风貌：圣母百花大教堂、乔托钟楼、巴杰罗美术馆、老桥等尽收眼底。这里也是观赏日出和日落的好位置，随着光线的变幻，看佛罗伦萨焕发出不同的光彩。

attractions.text_en: Cinque Terre is located in the coastal area of the Province of La Spezia in the Liguria region of Italy, built along the winding Mediterranean coastline. It is the collective name for these five clifftop villages and towns: Monterosso al Mare, Vernazza, Corniglia, Manarola, and Riomaggiore. What is special is the hiking route that strings the five villages together, built along the coast — it is one of the most beautiful hiking routes in the world, about 15-20 kilometers, taking about 5 hours; on one side is the Mediterranean, on the other side are the mountains, and ahead are the Italian fishing hamlets, making it a destination that many hiking enthusiasts flock to. In 1997 it was inscribed on the World Cultural Heritage List by UNESCO.
attractions.text_zh: 五渔村位于意大利利古里亚大区(Liguria)拉斯佩齐亚省海沿岸地区，沿着蜿蜒的地中海海岸线而建，是蒙特罗索（Monterosso al Mare）、韦尔纳扎（Vernazza）、科尔尼利亚（Corniglia）、马纳罗拉（Manarola）及里奥马焦雷（Riomaggiore）这五个悬崖边上的村镇的统称。特别的是把五个村庄串起的徒步线路，沿着海岸而建，是世界上美丽的徒步路线，约15-20公里，需5小时左右，一边是地中海，另一边是山脉，而前面则是意大利渔庄，是诸多徒步爱好者趋之若鹜的胜地。1997年被联合国教科文组织列入世界文化遗产名录。

attractions.text_en: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is the freestanding bell tower of the cathedral of the city of Pisa in Italy. It is located on the Piazza dei Miracoli (Square of Miracles) on the north side of the city of Pisa in the Province of Tuscany, Italy. It is a world-famous sight and one of Italy's symbols, and was selected as a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Legend has it that in the year 1590, Galileo conducted a free-fall experiment on the Leaning Tower of Pisa and overturned the view of Aristotle.
attractions.text_zh: 比萨斜塔是意大利比萨城大教堂的独立式钟楼，位于意大利托斯卡纳省比萨城北面的奇迹广场上，是世界著名的景观，也是意大利标志之一，被联合国教育科学文化组织评选为世界遗产。相传在公元1590年，伽利略曾经在比萨斜塔上做了一个自由落体实验，并推翻了亚里士多德的观点。

attractions.text_en: Also known as the Cathedral Square, it is a walled area in the center of the city of Pisa in the Tuscany region of Italy, part of which is grassland. The Piazza dei Miracoli is the center of the city of Pisa; on the square are four great buildings: the Pisa Cathedral, the Leaning Tower of Pisa (bell tower), the Baptistery of St. John, and the Camposanto (Pisa cemetery). Around the square, ancient walls and gates are also preserved. In April 1987, the International Council on Monuments and Sites nominated the Piazza dei Miracoli of the city of Pisa, Italy (including the cathedral, the baptistery, the Leaning Tower of Pisa, and the cemetery) as a World Heritage Site.
attractions.text_zh: 又名主教座堂广场，是意大利托斯卡纳大区比萨市中心一片围墙包围的区域，部分为草地。奇迹广场是比萨城的中心，广场上有四大建筑：比萨主教座堂、比萨斜塔（钟楼）、圣乔瓦尼洗礼堂、比萨墓园。广场周围还保留着古代的城墙和城门。1987年4月，国际古迹遗产理事会提名意大利比萨城的奇迹广场（包括大教堂、洗礼堂、比萨斜塔和墓园）为世界遗产。

attractions.text_en: Torre Grossa is a tower in San Gimignano that is open to the public, and is also the taller tower, with a total height of 54 meters, begun in the early 14th century. It was once part of the new Palazzo del Podestà; from the tower one can take in the beautiful scenery of San Gimignano and the hilly mountain area of Tuscany.
attractions.text_zh: 格罗萨塔楼是圣吉米亚诺中一个对公众开放的塔楼，也是较高的塔楼，总高为54米，始建于14世纪初。它曾是新的波德斯塔宫宫的一部分，塔楼可以一览圣吉米亚诺和托斯卡纳丘陵山区的美景。

attractions.text_en: Although this medieval tower city inscribed as a World Heritage Site is small, it concentrates the finest scenery of Tuscany. The Piazza della Cisterna is the absolute core; this triangular square is paved with cobblestones, surrounded by 13th-century stone towers and arcades, and the ancient well in the center was once the lifeline of the whole city. Standing here and looking up at the densely standing towers, you can directly feel where the title "Medieval Manhattan" comes from.
attractions.text_zh: 这座被列入世界遗产的中世纪塔城虽小，却浓缩了托斯卡纳最精华的景观。水井广场是绝对核心，这个三角形广场由鹅卵石铺就，四周环绕着13世纪的石塔与拱廊，中央的古井曾是全城命脉。站在这里仰视林立的塔楼，能直观感受“中世纪曼哈顿”的称号从何而来。

attractions.text_en: Siena, this medieval ancient city, is filled with masterpieces of artistic masters and medieval classical architecture. Wandering through Siena's ancient, crisscrossing flagstone alleys and the deep, secluded courtyards hidden everywhere, you seem to be placed in medieval Europe a thousand years ago, and every detail will bring you a pleasant surprise. For several centuries, this city has maintained an intact classical appearance, as if time had stood still here since the 14th century. In addition to its unique scenery and art collections, Siena also maintains a series of traditions, including the cooking and making of desserts. On your trip to Siena, do not forget to taste the local specialties — almond soft cakes ricciarelli, nut tarts panforte, peppery spiced nut cakes pan pepato, rice puddings budini di riso, walnut cookies cavallucci, or walnut wafers copate!
attractions.text_zh: 锡耶纳，这座中世纪古城布满了艺术大师的杰作和中世纪古典建筑，穿梭在锡耶纳古老而纵横交错的石板小巷和隐藏在各处的幽深庭院中，您仿佛置身于千年前的欧洲中世纪，其中的每一处细节都会给您带来惊喜。这座城市在几个世纪以来，一直保持着完整的古典外貌，仿佛时间自十四世纪起便在此停滞。除了它独特的风景和艺术典藏，锡耶纳还保持着一系列的传统，包括甜品的烹饪制作。在您的锡耶纳之行中，别忘了品尝当地的特色- 杏仁软饼ricciarelli、果仁馅饼panforte、胡椒香料果仁糕pan pepato、米饭布丁budini di riso、核桃饼cavallucci、或核桃华夫copate!

attractions.text_en: Piazza del Campo (also called the Shell Square because of its shell-like shape) is the center of the city of Siena. Directly facing the square are the Siena City Hall and its tower; inside the City Hall is the Civic Museum, which holds numerous paintings of the Sienese school, including Ambrogio Lorenzetti's "Allegory of Good Government" and "Allegory of Bad Government." The tower's full name is the Torre del Mangia; it is the iconic building of Siena, and also the second-tallest tower in Italy, with a vertical height reaching 102 meters. Visitors can reach the top via 55 flights of steps and take in the beautiful scenery of the city of Siena.
attractions.text_zh: 田野广场(因形似贝壳也叫贝壳广场)是锡耶纳城市的中心，广场正对面是锡耶纳市政厅和塔楼，市政厅内有市立博物馆，里面藏有锡耶纳学派的众多绘画作品，包括AmbrogioLorenzetti的《好政府寓意》和《坏政府寓意》。塔楼全名为曼贾塔TorrediMangia，它是锡耶纳标志性建筑，也是意大利第二高的塔楼，垂直高度达到102米，游客可以通过55层的阶梯达到顶端，一览锡耶纳城的美景。

attractions.text_en: Pienza, a small town on a hill in the Val d'Orcia in southern Tuscany, is the birthplace of Pope Pius II. In 1459 the Pienza he oversaw rebuilding was regarded as a model of the "ideal city," and so it is hailed as "the touchstone of Renaissance urban living." Unlike other Italian medieval towns, Pienza's unique feature is that from the city walls you can look down upon the scenery of the World Heritage "Val d'Orcia."
attractions.text_zh: 皮恩扎，位于托斯卡尼南部奥尔恰山谷山丘上的小镇，这里是教皇皮乌斯二世的出生地，1459年他主持重建的皮恩扎被视为“理想城市”的典范，因此被誉为“文艺复兴都市生活的试金石”。与其他意大利中世纪小镇不同，皮恩扎独特之处在于可以从城墙上俯瞰世界遗产-「Val D'orcia奥尔恰山谷」的景致。

attractions.text_en: The winding cypress path, a country road lined on both sides with Italian cypresses, with its uniquely shaped curves and neatly arranged cypress trees, has become one of the symbols of Tuscan pastoral scenery. The cypresses on both sides of the road cast dappled shadows under the sunshine, creating a tranquil and poetic atmosphere that attracts countless photography enthusiasts and tourists to come and take photos.
attractions.text_zh: 蜿蜒曲折丝柏树小径、两旁种满意大利柏树的乡村道路，独特造型的弯道和整齐排列的丝柏树，成为托斯卡纳田园风光的象征之一。道路两侧的柏树在阳光的照射下投下斑驳的影子，营造出一种宁静而诗意的氛围，吸引了无数摄影爱好者和游客前来打卡。

attractions.text_en: The spectacular Colosseum, located in the center of Rome, was built by ancient Rome at the time to please the generals and soldiers returning in triumph and to extol the great ancient Roman Empire. It was a place exclusively for slave owners, nobles, and freemen of ancient Rome to watch beast fights or gladiatorial combat by slaves, and is a famous historical relic of Rome. The Colosseum can be called a model masterpiece and a marvel in architectural history, renowned throughout the world for being huge, majestic, and magnificent. Although only a little more than half of its skeleton remains, its majestic spirit and grand momentum still endure.
attractions.text_zh: 位于罗马中心的壮观的斗兽场是古罗马当时为取悦凯旋而归的将领士兵和赞美伟大的古罗马帝国而建造的，是古罗马帝国专供奴隶主、贵族和自由民观看斗兽或奴隶角斗的地方,是罗马著名的历史遗迹。斗兽场在建筑史上堪称典范的杰作和奇迹，以庞大、雄伟、壮观著称于世。虽只剩下大半个骨架，但其雄伟之气魄、磅礴之气势犹存。

attractions.text_en: The Pantheon is a well-preserved building of ancient Rome, with a history of more than 2,000 years to this day. The artist Raphael, the Roman king Vittorio Emanuele II, and Umberto I, among others, rest in eternal sleep here. The Pantheon was built by Agrippa, son-in-law of Octavian, the first emperor of the Roman Empire, to enshrine the gods of Mount Olympus. It can be called a classic building of the Augustan period and a model of the consummate building technology of ancient Rome, praised by Michelangelo as "the design of angels."
attractions.text_zh: 万神殿是古罗马保存完好的建筑，距今已有2000多年的历史，艺术家拉斐尔、罗马皇帝维托里奥﹒埃曼努埃尔二世和温布尔一世等长眠于此。万神殿由罗马帝国首任皇帝屋大维的女婿阿戈利巴建造，用以供奉奥林匹亚山上诸神，可谓奥古斯都时期的经典建筑，是古罗马精湛建筑技术的典范，被米开朗基罗赞叹为“天使的设计”。

attractions.text_en: Piazza di Spagna once appeared in scenes of the film "Roman Holiday," becoming a calling card of Rome known to everyone. Audrey Hepburn's fresh and bright figure seems to have already merged into one with the Piazza di Spagna and beautiful Rome, becoming a memory that people can never erase. The whole Piazza di Spagna is not large in area, and the crowds mainly gather near the Spanish Steps. The entire flight of steps has 138 steps in total, and is one of the wider flights of steps in Europe.
attractions.text_zh: 西班牙广场曾经出现在电影《罗马假日》的场景中，成为了所有人都知道的罗马名片。赫本那清新亮丽的倩影似乎已经与西班牙广场以及美丽的罗马合为一体，成为了人们永远挥之不去的记忆。西班牙台阶整个西班牙广场面积不大，人群主要集中在西班牙台阶附近。整个台阶共138级，是欧洲较宽的台阶。

attractions.name_zh: 特莱维喷泉(许愿池)（外观）
attractions.text_en: Also known as the "Maiden Fountain," or the "Wishing Pool," it takes as its theme the triumphant return of Neptune, god of the sea in Roman mythology, with an overall imposing grandeur and magnificence. It is said that if you stand with your back to the fountain and toss a coin over your shoulder, and it lands in the water, your dream can come true.
attractions.text_zh: 又称“少女喷泉”，或者“许愿池”，以罗马神话中海神尼普勒战胜归来为题材，整气势磅礴、大气恢宏。据说背对着喷泉、从肩上投出一枚硬币，如果能投进水中，就能梦想成真。

attractions.text_en: If the Louvre bears witness to the glory of the Gauls, then the Île de la Cité, Notre-Dame de Paris and the Hôtel de Ville de Paris record the indissoluble bond with the Romans who conquered them. Without the Romans, there would be no Île de la Cité. Without the Île de la Cité, there would be no Paris as it is today.
attractions.text_zh: 如果说卢浮宫见证了高卢人的荣耀，那西岱岛，巴黎圣母院和巴黎市政厅则写下了与征服她们的罗马人的不解之缘。如果没有罗马人，就没有西岱岛。没有西岱岛，就没有现在的巴黎。

attractions.text_en: The Cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris is located on the Île de la Cité in the heart of Paris and is the cathedral of the Catholic Archdiocese of Paris. Built in the Gothic style, it allows you to take a close-up look at its appearance during the reconstruction following the great fire.
attractions.text_zh: 巴黎圣母院大教堂坐落于巴黎市中心的西堤岛上，是天主教巴黎总教区的主教座堂。教堂为哥特式风格，可近距离一睹历经大火后重建中的风貌。

attractions.text_en: It is one of the oldest museums in the world and a world-renowned treasure house of art. The Louvre building is shaped like a "U", divided into a new part and an old part. The entrance at the glass pyramid in front of the Louvre was designed by the master Chinese-American architect I. M. Pei. Here are treasured 25,000 art treasures, with as many as over 400,000 exhibits, encompassing artworks from ancient Egypt, Greece, Etruria and Rome to the European Middle Ages and the countries of the East. It can be called the greatest palace of art in human history.
attractions.text_zh: 是世界上古老的博物馆之一，是一座举世闻名的艺术宝库。卢浮宫建筑呈“U”形，分别为新、旧两个部分。卢浮宫前玻璃金字塔的入口是华裔建筑大师贝聿铭所设计。在这里珍藏着2.5万件艺术珍品，展品更是多达40余万件，囊括了古埃及、希腊、伊特鲁里亚、罗马到欧洲中世纪和东方各国的艺术品，堪称人类历史上大的艺术殿堂。

attractions.text_en: Located in the middle section of the Champs-Élysées, on the north bank of the Seine, it is the most renowned square in France. The square was built in the 18th century, originally constructed by order of Louis XV to proclaim his supreme imperial power. Originally named "Place de la Révolution", it witnessed the dark and bloody history before and after the French Revolution and the people's tenacious struggle.
attractions.text_zh: 位于香榭丽舍大道中段，塞纳河北岸，是法国最负盛名的广场。广场建于18世纪，最初由路易十五下令营建，以昭示自己至高无上的皇权。原名为”革命广场“，见证了法国大革命前后的黑暗血腥的历史与人民的顽强抗争。

attractions.text_en: The Champ de Mars is a large strip-shaped park located in the 7th arrondissement of Paris, France, lying between the Eiffel Tower to its northwest and the École Militaire of Paris to its southeast. Before the latter half of the 18th century, this was an abandoned plot of empty land. It was not until the École Militaire was established in 1765 that its status gradually rose, when its main purpose was to serve as a place for military training. During the French Revolution, on 17 July 1791, a massacre and bloodshed once took place here. Later, France used this site to host several World Expositions as well as the 1900 Olympic Games.
attractions.text_zh: 战神广场是一个座落于法国巴黎七区的广大带状公园，介于位在其西北方的埃菲尔铁塔、以及在其东南方的巴黎军校之间。 在十八世纪下半叶之前，这是一块被遗弃的空地，直到军事学校在1765年建立，它的地位才逐渐提升，当时主要的目的是用来作为军事训练的场所。在法国大革命时期的1791年7月17日，此处曾发生屠杀流血事件。后来法国曾经使用这个场地迎接过几次的世界博览会以及1900年的奥林匹克运动会。

attractions.text_en: The Eiffel Tower is one of the symbols of Paris, affectionately called the "Iron Lady" by the French. If Notre-Dame de Paris is the symbol of ancient Paris, then the Eiffel Tower is the symbol of modern Paris.
attractions.text_zh: 埃菲尔铁塔是巴黎的标志之一，被法国人爱称为“铁娘子”。如果说，巴黎圣母院是古代巴黎的象征，那么埃菲尔铁塔就是现代巴黎的标志。

attractions.text_en: Take the famous "Bateaux Mouches" cruise boats with transparent cabins, sailing along the banks of the Seine which blends together numerous sights. Over a 3.2-kilometre journey of about 1 hour, it passes such famous landmarks as Notre-Dame de Paris, the Grand and Petit Palais, the Pont Alexandre III, the Paris lock bridge, the Louvre, the Champs-Élysées and the Île de la Cité. On the surface of the Seine you can feel the romantic night scenery of Paris, the beautiful views and the wonderful music — it is called "the in-depth tour of Paris not to be missed".
attractions.text_zh: 乘坐著名的透明船舱的“苍蝇船”游船航行在融合诸多景点的塞纳河畔，3.2公里约1小时的航程途径巴黎圣母院，大小皇宫、亚历山大三世大桥，巴黎锁桥，卢浮宫、香榭丽舍大道巴黎西黛岛等著名景点，在塞纳河面上感受巴黎浪漫夜色，美景及美妙的音乐，被称为《巴黎不可错过的深度游览项目》。

attractions.text_en: The Palace of Versailles has always been a magnificent monument of the period of France's feudal rule. In terms of content it was not only the French court, but also the administrative centre of the state, and a concrete embodiment of the social and political views and the way of life of France at that time. It was the first time since the ancient Roman Empire that Europe demonstrated the power of an autocratic regime capable of concentrating such enormous manpower, material resources and financial resources. At that time, in order to build it, Louis XIV employed more than thirty thousand workers as well as architects, engineers and technicians. Apart from having to solve the complex technical problems arising from constructing a large-scale building complex, they also had to solve problems in all aspects such as water supply and roads. It can be seen that the success of the Palace of Versailles powerfully proves the progress of France's economy and technology at that time and the wisdom of the labouring people.
attractions.text_zh: 凡尔赛宫始终是法国封建统治历史时期的一座华丽的纪念碑。从内容上讲不仅是法兰西宫廷，而且是国家的行政中心，也是当时法国社会政治观点、生活方式的具体体现。它是欧洲自古罗马帝国以来，第一次表现出能够集中如此巨大的人力、物力、财力的专制政体力量。当时，路易十四为了建造它，共动用了三万余名工人和建筑师、工程师、技师，除了要解决建造大规模建筑群所产生的复杂技术问题外，还要解决引水、道路等各方面的问题。可见，凡尔赛宫的成功，有力地证明了当时法国经济和技术的进步和劳动人民的智慧。

attractions.text_en: Boulevard Haussmann lies between the 8th and 9th arrondissements of Paris. On the street are the two great department stores of Paris, Galeries Lafayette and Printemps. Marcel Proust, the author of "In Search of Lost Time", once completed the greater part of the writing of "In Search of Lost Time" in his residence at No. 102 on this street.
attractions.text_zh: 奥斯曼大道位于巴黎第八区和第九区之间。街上有巴黎老佛爷百货公司和春天百货两大百货公司。《追忆似水年华》的作者马塞尔·普鲁斯特曾在这条街102号的居所里，完成了《追忆似水年华》的大部分写作。

attractions.text_en: The Paris Opera was designed by Charles Garnier in 1861. It is a consummate masterpiece of eclecticism, its architecture perfectly combining several architectural forms such as the ancient Greek and Roman colonnade and the Baroque. Grand in scale, exquisite and meticulous, resplendent and magnificent, it is acclaimed as a theatre in which painting, marble and gilded ornament shine upon one another, giving people the greatest enjoyment. It is one of the typical buildings of Napoleon III.
attractions.text_zh: 巴黎歌剧院是由查尔斯·加尼叶于1861年设计的，是折衷主义登峰造极的作品，其建筑将古希腊罗马式柱廊、巴洛克等几种建筑形式完美地结合在一起，规模宏大，精美细致，金碧辉煌，被誉为是一座绘画、大理石和金饰交相辉映的剧院，给人以极大的享受，是拿破仑三世典型的建筑之一。
